Subway Surfers er et platformspil til telefon udviklet af de danske firmaer Kiloo og SYBO Games. Spillet blev først udsendt i maj 2012, og er siden blevet en stor succes.
I 2017, og igen i 2021, var Subway Surfers det mest downloadede spil i verden.
I spillet er spilleren en ung graffitikunstner, som da personen bliver fanget i at "tagge" et tog, løber gennem jernbaneskinnerne for at undslippe.
Hver måned er der i gennemsnit 100 millioner, der spiller Subway surfers, og hver dag bliver spillet downloadet over én million gange. I perioder med corona-nedlukning steg antallet til halvanden million. I otte år har en samarbejdsaftale mellem to danske spilfirmaer, Kiloo i Aarhus og Sybo i København, dannet grundlag for den største, danske kommercielle spilsucces nogensinde: Mobilspillet Subway Surfers, hvor en graffitiglad fyr hopper rundt mellem jernbaneskinner og vagtfolk - og tjener et milliardbeløb hjem til skaberne. Men i januar udløb aftalen, og derfor bliver der nu lavet om på samarbejdet. Spillet er udviklet sammen af de to spilproducenter, men med skillelinjer. Sybo ejer alle rettigheder til selve spillets indhold, altså IP-rettighederne, og Kiloo har haft rollen som udgiver af spillet.
Da spillet udkom, forklarede Kiloos kreative direktør det selv sådan, at Sybo stod for oplevelsen med spillet, og Kiloo for alt det ’uden om’.
Men fremover bliver det ændret, så Sybo kommer til at overtage udgivelsen på mobile platforme. Til gengæld skal Kiloo nu udgive spillet på andre platforme, for eksempel som webspil og hos flyselskaber.
Referencer

Subway Surfers is a 2012 endless runner mobile game which is co-developed by Kiloo and SYBO Games, private companies based in Denmark. It is available on iOS, Android, HarmonyOS NEXT, Amazon Fire Tablet, Windows Phone platforms, and is playable on web browser at Poki.com and uses the Unity game engine.
In the game, players take the role of young graffiti artists who, upon being caught in the act of tagging a metro railway site, run through the railroad tracks to escape from a police officer. As they run, the player must avoid colliding with train cars and other objects while collecting coins and power-ups.
Subway Surfers received mixed reviews. A sequel, Subway Surfers City, released on February 26, 2026, featuring new characters, mechanics and locations.
Gameplay
Subway Surfers is an endless runner video game. The game starts by tapping the screen, while Jake (the game's main character) or any other character sprays graffiti on a subway, and gets caught in the act by the inspector (police officer) and his dog, who starts chasing the character. While running, the player can swipe up, down, left, or right to avoid crashing into oncoming obstacles especially moving trains, poles, tunnel walls, and barriers. By swiping rapidly as speed increases, more points can be acquired. A crash results in a game over, but the player can revive and continue by using keys or watching an ad.
The player can collect various items such as coins, keys, x2 multipliers, super sneakers, jetpacks, magnets, mystery boxes, and pogo sticks. A pogo stick provides propulsion by launching the character upward, a jetpack gives the ability to fly, a coin magnet attracts coins on the track, super sneakers give the ability to jump higher, and a x2 multiplier multiplies the rate at which the score counts. Items, such as a hoverboard (which could be used by double-tapping on the screen), allow the character to avoid collisions and lasts about 30 seconds.
Daily Challenges and Season Hunts give rewards for unique movements throughout gameplay. In daily challenges, the player needs to collect letters that constitute a word related to the game, such as "score" and "jump"; also known as "word hunt". Missions have various tasks measured by player accuracy. Up to 18 characters can be unlocked using coins, keys, in-game purchases, collecting specific items, or connecting to a Facebook account. Most characters have up to 2 different outfits. Meanwhile, up to 17 hoverboards can be unlocked with the same methods. Each has different abilities that can assist the player. When the game updates to a new location, a new character, and hoverboard will be available temporarily until the next update.
Special events, such as the Season Hunt, including the game's birthday events, the Super Runner Challenge and Rivals Challenge, can result in in-game rewards and characters. Additional outfits and characters can be purchased with coins and keys as well.
Release
Subway Surfers was released on 24 May 2012 with updates based on seasonal holidays. Since January 2013, updates have been based on a "World Tour" theme, which updates the setting of the game every three (or four, usually for seasonal holidays) weeks.
In March 2018, Subway Surfers became the first game on the Google Play Store to cross the one billion downloads threshold. In May 2018, Subway Surfers crossed the two billion downloads mark. App Annie reported Subway Surfers as the #2 downloaded game of all time in iOS App Store.
In December 2019, SYBO Games announced that Subway Surfers, according to AppAnnie statistics, crossed the 2.7 billion download mark. Subway Surfers was the most downloaded mobile game of the decade from 2012 to 2019. A spin-off, Subway Surfers Tag, was released exclusively on Apple Arcade in 2022.
Reception
Subway Surfers received mixed reviews. Critics praised the game's visual style and entertaining gameplay but criticized it for its monotonous world and unresponsive controls. Review aggregator website Metacritic gave the game 71 out of 100 based on 8 reviews.
Dan Griliopoulos of Pocket Gamer gave the game a score of 5 out of 10, praising the game's fun gameplay and free access, but criticizing the game's controls and parsimonious game design.
Gamezebo's Dant Rambo scored the game 3.5 out of 5, writing "It makes little attempt to stand out from other endless runners, but it's hard not to appreciate the polish of Subway Surfers. The controls are responsive, the gameplay is addictive, and it doesn't try and force you into spending cash on in-game items."
Other reviewers were not as critical. 148Apps and TouchArcade gave the game 4/5. The game was nominated for "Action Game" and "Family & Kids Game" at the 2019 Webby Awards.
Legacy
Adaptation
On 1 June 2018, a series of animated shorts debuted on SYBO Games' YouTube channel entitled Subway Surfers: The Animated Series. The 10 x 4-minute episodic series is scripted by Brent Friedman and Francesca Marie Smith, and produced by Sander Schwartz. Chris Bartleman is the supervising director and Michael Hegner is the director. Denmark's William provides post-production services. The series has 11 episodes.
Use in sludge content
The game has been a favorite among "sludge content" creators, possibly due to its highway hypnosis-like visuals.

10th Birthday
10th Birthday is a hoverboard of the game launched on . It was given for free to the player in .
Appearance
It's a pink and yellow striped board wrapped in a blue bow.
Trivia
This is the fourth hoverboard to celebrate Subway Surfers birthday. The first being 8th Birthday , the second being 9th Birthday , the fourth being 11th Birthday , the fifth being 12th Birthday , and the sixth being 13th Birthday .
This is the fifth free hoverboard. The first being the default hoverboard, the second being Starboard , the third being 8th Birthday , and the fourth being 9th Birthday .
It's the 8th board of 2022 , after Super Surfer , Crash Test , Rolls Rabbit , Spring , Astronave , Lunar Tiger , and Atomic Tile .
It is the 1st board of 2022 that did not return after its release yet, the 2nd was Wrapped .

13th Birthday
13th Birthday is a hoverboard released in . Like any other hoverboard, it protects the character from crashing for 30 seconds. When crashed, it will need to recharge for a few seconds before it can be used again.

Trivia
This year marks the 1st anniversary of Subway Surfers , and the beginning of the World Tour .
This is the only year where there are no World Tour editions between the Halloween and Christmas editions.
This is the only year where every edition is a new edition.
This, 2022 , and 2023 are currently the only years where every new edition has been re-released at some point.

was released.

was released.

A bugfix for the update came out.

was released. This is the first edition to be released three times.

was released.

Updates Debuts
Trivia
This year marks the 5th anniversary of Subway Surfers, and the 4rd anniversary of the World Tour.
This year introduced remixed Background Music , which was first used in . Some editions would either have or not have remixed background music, until 2020 ( ), where every edition onwards has remixed background music (except 3.29.1 and ).
Currently this is the only year:
To have the first world tour located in Europe continent ( ).
Where all surfers receive two outfits (with Lee received his second outfit in later).
To have the first world tour located in Europe continent ( ).
Where all surfers receive two outfits (with Lee received his second outfit in later).

was released.

Updates Debuts
Video
Trivia
If one does count Buddy as a 2015 surfer then this is the 1st year without a a new Christmas surfer.
This year had the least amount of new World Tour surfers, tied with 2015 if one counts Boombot .
Without Boombot, this is the year that released the fewest restricted surfers, even less than 2015.
Without Boombot, this is the year that released the fewest restricted surfers, even less than 2015.
This is the only year that the FIFA World Cup was not held (which was held in Russia).

Trivia
This year marks the 8th anniversary of the game .
This year marks the 7th anniversary of the World Tour .
75% of the new updates released in 2020 were located in USA (3/4).
This year and 2024 are the only ones (so far) of 2020s decade to not celebrate the game’s anniversary in a re-release of .
So far, this is the last year where its first edition ( ) was released in the previous year .

Trivia
This year marks the 9th aniversary of the game .
This year marks the 8th anniversary of the World Tour , and 9 years of Subway Surfers .
Prior to 2024 , this year introduced the most new surfers in Subway Surfers history with 30 new surfers.
Prior to 2025 , this is the last year to introduced some of the new characters were released along with a new outfit for them ( Qin 's Warrior Outfit; Hou 's Archer Outfit).
This is the last year to introduce a character costing .

Trivia
This year marks the 10th aniversary of the game .
This year marks the 9th anniversary of the World Tour , and 10 years of Subway Surfers .
Ever since , every update has been one day before a 2018 and 2021 edition (since for 2018 updates).
This is the first year of the 2020 's decade to not bring a character from the Chinese Version.
This is the first (and currently only) year where not a single 2013 edition is re-released.
This is the first year where there are the same amount of character as a previous year .
and were released in opposite order of there 2014 counterparts.
This is the second year the Easter hunt token is not a (instead it being a ) the first being 2019 with .
This is the first year where every edition is unplayable.
This is the second time a city is being re-released in the same year, the first being 2015 , with and .
This is the first and second time a city we're meant to be re-released in the same year and gets re-released.
This is also the second time a city that was released that year got re-released on the same year.
This is the first and second time a city we're meant to be re-released in the same year and gets re-released.
This is also the second time a city that was released that year got re-released on the same year.
This year has two re-releases ( and ) and two 's ( and ).
Since v2.30.1/v2.30.2 Characters have started to lose HD Graphics (even though they get graphics instead of - graphics), as of the all characters lost their HD Graphics.
This is the first (and currently only) year to only include one USA edition ( ).
It also happens to be a re-release.
It also happens to be a re-release.

Trivia
This year marks the 11th aniversary of the game .
This year marks the 10th anniversary of the World Tour , and 11 years of Subway Surfers .
This year released the same amount of characters as 2020 and 2022 .
Like 2022 , characters continue losing rim light. This oddly has not been fixed for most surfers except for Bob The Blob and Captain Flash .
However as of the 3.32.3, all characters lost rim light, including Captain Flash .
Bob The Blob had already lost rim light again in .
However as of the 3.32.3, all characters lost rim light, including Captain Flash .
Bob The Blob had already lost rim light again in .
This is the 1st year since 2016 where the 1st hunt token is not a new hunt token.
This year fixed some characters. Bob The Blob 's rim light was fixed, Maeko , Dylan and Hali 's expressions were fixed and Noel , Miss Maia , Pride Miss Maia , Olivia , Dylan , and Alicia 's eye movement was fixed.
This is the first year where the Lunar New Year edition is the first World Tour of the year and not the second.
This is the first year where  wasn't released alongside .
This is the first year since 2021 ( ) where a trailer was released on the day as the update .
This is the first year since 2015 ( ) to not have a new special surfer in the first release, being .
This is also the 1st year since 2020 ( ) to not release a new special surfer.
This is also the 1st year since 2020 ( ) to not release a new special surfer.
This is the first year in the 2020's decade to not release 4 new Locations.
This is also the 1st year to not release a new location based on a real place.
This is also the 1st year to not release a new location based on a real place.
This is the first year since 2020 where a South American edition was re-released.
This year didn't re-release any location in Africa.
It's also the first year since 2019 to not re-release any location in Africa.
It's also the first year since 2019 to not re-release any location in Africa.
This is the 1st year since 2016 where Outfits were given to Characters in every update.
This is the 2nd year of the 2020's decade to not bring a character from the Chinese Version.
This is the 1st year where all the United Kingdom editions were re-released ( , and ).
This is the 1st year since 2020 to only include one China edition ( 2023).
This is the 2nd year to feature the special events that celebrates the game's existence ( Rivals event, released in May 2023), the first being Super Runners (celebrates 10 years).

Trivia
This year marks the 12th anniversary of Subway Surfers , and the 11th anniversary of the World Tour .
This is the first year to introduce at least two characters and hoverboards in each World Tour location.
This year releases two new game modes:
The first is , becoming the second No Floor Challenge game mode series after , which was caused by Yutani 's clumsy actions.
The second is , where the character has to collect candies dropped by Bob The Blob 's Slime Outfit.

and were the only World Tour Editions where no characters got new outfits.

To have a single character ( Alfie ) receive two outfits in the same year.

Trivia
This year marks the 13th anniversary of Subway Surfers , and the 12th anniversary of the World Tour .
This year currently holds the record for the most amount of surfers being released (47), surpassing 2024 (40).
This is the third year in a row to only include one Chinese edition ( ).
This is the first year since 2017 to not release a new robot character in the game.
This is the first year since 2020 where both Lunar New Year and Saint Valentine's Day were independent updates.
This is the first year since 2021 where some of the new characters were released along with a new outfit for them.
This is the first year since 2022 where some of the new characters received a new outfit in a few updates after the release of their default outfit in the same year.
This is the first year since 2023 :
To have an edition not releasing a new special surfer.
To release a South American and Latin American update.
To have a Hispanic update.
To have an edition not releasing a new special surfer.
To release a South American and Latin American update.
To have a Hispanic update.
This is the first year to introduce the fourth outfit for a limited character ( with Bonnie 's Thumper Outfit).
This is the first year where was not released alongside .
This year didn't re-release any location in Africa.
It's also the first year since 2023 to not re-release any location in Africa.
It's also the first year since 2023 to not re-release any location in Africa.
This is the second year in the 2020's decade to not release 4 new Locations.
This is the first year to have only one legacy update ( ).
More than half of the editions this year were destinations that were last visited in 2023 . (9 out of 17)
Starting from this year, at least one or more characters / outfits / boards cost .
Akira 's Night Rider outfit and Override in

Festive Jake 's Jester Outfit in
Since , the content always has a 2+ Crew Bonus , making it impossible to max out the Crew Bonus without paying real money, even for veteran players.
However, starting in , the content five editions ago would be re-released without costing , starting with Akira 's Night Rider Outfit (except Override which so far, costed only in and , and collab characters like Spike and Yutani Tree Frog , who were skipped).

Festive Jake 's Jester Outfit in
Since , the content always has a 2+ Crew Bonus , making it impossible to max out the Crew Bonus without paying real money, even for veteran players.
However, starting in , the content five editions ago would be re-released without costing , starting with Akira 's Night Rider Outfit (except Override which so far, costed only in and , and collab characters like Spike and Yutani Tree Frog , who were skipped).
Since , the content always has a 2+ Crew Bonus , making it impossible to max out the Crew Bonus without paying real money, even for veteran players.
However, starting in , the content five editions ago would be re-released without costing , starting with Akira 's Night Rider Outfit (except Override which so far, costed only in and , and collab characters like Spike and Yutani Tree Frog , who were skipped).

Trivia
This year marks the 14th anniversary of Subway Surfers , and the 13th anniversary of the World Tour .
This is the first year since 2022 where the first update doesn’t take place in China.
Since Meet the Crew introduction in 2023, this is the first year to introduce at least one new surfer for each Zodiac Signs .
Gallery
Navigation

2X Multiplier
2x Multiplier is a power-up in Subway Surfers . When picked up, the score multiplier is doubled, allowing the player to get double the amount of points in a specific period of time. For instance, if the score multiplier is X5, it will be X10 when the power-up is picked up. The multiplier number also moderately flashes in green and white.
Appearance
It is a yellow to orange gradient five-pointed star with a thick inner golden brown border and ridges on the sides and '2x' written on it.
It is now mono colored in a mutated orange-yellow, and no longer has ridges on the sides.
Trivia
With this, plus the Score Booster and the Hot Rod board or the Daredevil board , and upgrades in some other boards, scores can increase at insanely high speeds.
The 2X Multiplier, the Score Booster, the Super Mysterizer, and the missions are the only mechanics in the game that can affect the score multiplier.
Strangely, the icon of the 2x multiplier is written as '2x', while the one to be collected on the tracks is written as 'x2'.
This and the Score Booster are the only power-ups not seen held by the player's  character when used, which is probably because they can not be physically held. It is also the only power-up that does not physically affect the player's gameplay.
Its current design has been used in and so on.
Gallery
Navigation

4th of July
4th of July  is a limited  hoverboard  introduced in the  . Like any other hoverboard, it will protect you from crashing for 30 seconds. When crashed, the board will explode and recharge for a few seconds before it can be used again. It has two upgrades, the first being  Spark Trail , which costs  30 and the 2nd being  Super Speed , which costs  50. 4th of July itself costs .
Appearance
It has the appearance of a Rocket with the colors of the United States flag and has a trail of fireworks to celebrate the Independence Day of USA on 4th July. Equipping the Spark Trail upgrade makes the fuse start sparking. The Super Speed upgrade adds four fins to the board.
Trivia
It has a similar 3D mesh and has the powers of the Rocket board.
It has the same price as the Rocket board.
In , this board appears on Wild Wednesdays for on July 15, 2020.
In , it came back for 50.
This is the first Hoverboard to be named after a number.
Unlike Rocket , it has not been used in 2022 and 2023 for unknown reasons.
In , it made its first comeback since 2021 for .
In , it made its first comeback since 2021 for .
In , it costs .
In , it costs .

8-bit Aliens
8-bit Aliens are the  Weekly Hunt  tokens that are needed to be collected during the  update.
Appearance
They have the appearance of the aliens that feature on Space Invaders. It celebrates the Game On Expo 2019 .
Prizes
Week 1: 10 for 2
Week 2: 20 for 5
Week 3+4: 40 for 2
Trivia
This is the second token. The first is Sea Lions ( ), the third is Rainbows ( ), and the fourth is Sunnies ( ).
For unknown reasons, this token didn’t appear in . Instead it was replaced with Elephants ( ).
Gallery

8 Ball
8 Ball is a limited hoverboard released in . Like any other hoverboard, it protects the character from crashing for 30 seconds. When crashed, it will need to recharge for a few seconds before it can be used again.
Appearance
A black hoverboard with the 8 Ball Pool logo.
Trivia
It, The De-lyre-ious , and Moon Biter are the 36th, 37th, and 38th boards released in 2025 , after Hyper Viper , Lantern , Override , Fresh Love , Heartstopper , Dear Dart , Roarsome , Arara Board , Moongazer , Purple Spin , Cyan Note , Blue Riff , Eggscalibur , Bloomster , Edinburst , Guard Board , Trespasser , Mizu Dash , Zapline , Seven Seas , 13th Birthday , You Are Here , Freebird , Glitter Blaster , Float-Booster , Feeling Beachy , Street Style , Roll & Race , Midsummer , Business Time , Rabbit Racer , Circuit Bunny , E-Racer , Brainstormer , and Principal's Nightmare .
It's 8 Ball Jake 's board .
Gallery
Navigation

8 Ball Jake
8 Ball Jake is a character released during . He is the 243rd character in the game and the 28th surfer of 2025 .
Appearance
Default Outfit
8 Ball Jake is basically Jake but wearing a flame biker jacket instead of his usual denim jacket. There is also an 8-Ball on the back of it.
Trivia
He and Sunny are the 27th and 28th surfers released in 2025 , after Snake , Tang Yuan , Valentina , Kaleo , Rebeca Roar , Gabriel , Dinorange , Rocky , Rani , Finlay , Ribbon , Chicken Jake , Mallard Tricky , Oni Hime , Saru , LOC , Stevie , Berta , Costa , Yutani Tree Frog , Rochelle Rebelle , Asher , White Rabbit , Peter , Tara , and Henry .
He is the fifth surfer after Nikos , Moira , Azura and Sunny .
He is the ninth limited variant of Jake , after Zombie Jake , Festive Jake , Pride Jake , Super Runner Jake , Elf Jake , Outatime Jake , Pixel Jake , and Chicken Jake .
He is also the second game collaboration variant of Jake (for 8 Ball Pool ), after Chicken Jake (for Crossy Road ).
He is also the second game collaboration variant of Jake (for 8 Ball Pool ), after Chicken Jake (for Crossy Road ).
He is the first surfer who’s name starts with a number instead of a letter (although this is just a variant for Jake ).
8 Ball is his board .
Navigation

8 Ball Jake Illustration Portrait
8 Ball Jake Illustration Portrait is a limited portrait released in the update. This portrait can be unlocked by collecting 5 in the event during .
Appearance
This portrait is basically 8 Ball Jake but it is illustrated with a green background.
Trivia
This is the 28th portrait released in 2025 , after Akira Illustration Portrait , Noodlebox Graffiti Portrait , Valentina Illustration Portrait , Pink Guard Graffiti Portrait , Rebeca Roar Illustration Portrait , Fantasma Graffiti Portrait , Yutani Graffiti Portrait , Rocky Illustration Portrait , Astra Graffiti Portrait , Chicken Jake Illustration Portrait , Soul Illustration Portrait , Meggan Graffiti Portrait , Oni Hime Illustration Portrait , Dog Graffiti Portrait , Stevie Illustration Portrait , Tricky Graffiti Portrait , Berta Illustration Portrait , Fresh Graffiti Portrait , Miss Maia Illustration Portrait , Rochelle Rebelle Graffiti Portrait , Guard Illustration Portrait , White Rabbit Illustration Portrait , Guard Graffiti Portrait , Tara Graffiti Portrait , George Illustration Portrait , Sunny Graffiti Portrait , and Skateboard Graffiti Portrait .

8th Birthday
8th Birthday (formerly known as Birthday ) is a hoverboard that was released with . It is the fifty-second limited hoverboard in Subway Surfers . Like any other hoverboard, it will protect you from crashing for 30 seconds. When crashed, the board will explode and recharge for a few seconds before it can be used again. This is a free gift to celebrate Subway Surfers ' 5th anniversary.
Appearance
It has the appearance of a pink and purple birthday present with a gold ribbon.
Trivia
8th Birthday was originally called Birthday before .
It's strange that it's called 8th Birthday, because this board debuted on , the World Tour celebrating the game's 5th birthday.
However, it can refer to the games 8th birthday.
However, it can refer to the games 8th birthday.
It is the third free hoverboard. The first being the default hoverboard and the second being Starboard .
It is the first hoverboard to look like a present, the second is 9th Birthday , the third is 10th Birthday , the fourth is Wrapped , the fifth being 11th Birthday , the sixth is 12th Birthday , the seventh is 13th Birthday , and the eighth is 14th Birthday . It is the only one that has been re-released.
This is one of the few hoverboards in the game to have a trail upgrade that isn't listed as part of the board.
It is Freya 's first board. Her second and third boards are Hammer and Viking .
According to Bundle offers, Prince K , Tony , Kim , Roberto , Olivia , Jamie , Amira , Marco , Jenny , Jaro , Freya , Diego , Nina , Dylan , Miss Maia , Ace , Liu , and Ming share this board.
In the Chinese Version, it's called Anniversary (纪念日) .

9th Birthday
9th Birthday is a limited hoverboard in Subway Surfers exclusive to . Like any other hoverboard, it will protect you from crashing for 30 seconds. When crashed, the board will explode and recharge for a few seconds before it can be used again. It has the appearance of a birthday present. This is a free gift to celebrate Subway Surfers 9th anniversary.
Appearance
It's a blue and light blue-striped board with a yellow bow wrapped around it.
Trivia
This is the 10th board of 2021 after Secret Society , Endeavour , Discovery , Crane , Buddy Bear , Uprock , Bonnie , Shuttle and Viking .
It is a recolor of 8th Birthday .
The only difference between the two is that 9th Birthday is blue colored and it doesn't have the Confetti Trail like 8th Birthday .

This is the fourth free hoverboard. The first being the default hoverboard, the second being Starboard and the third being 8th Birthday .

Appearance
Default Outfit

Pilot Outfit
Launched in / , he wears a dark blue cap and sunglasses. He wears a blue button-down shirt with a dark blue tie, a dark blue shoulder strap, and a gold watch on his right wrist. He also has dark blue trousers with a belt and boots.

Akira
Akira is a limited character released in the update. He could be unlocked with 25,000 in . His 1st outfit, the Night Rider Outfit costs 150. After his release, he reappeared for every time, even after he got a new outfit.
Appearance
Default Outfit
Akira is a Japanese teenage boy and street racer from . He has black hair and yellow eyes. He wears a red hoodie, black basketball shorts over athletic leggings, yellow shoes with black laces, his mouth is covered by a black bandanna and he also has a black bag with a gray zipper.
Night Rider Outfit
Launched in , he wears a racing-style helmet, a dark gray and black armored jacket, and shorts with a red or reddish hue. The helmet features a white and purple/dark-purple design. The armored jacket has noticeable patterns and details. His boots/athletic shoes are a dark gray/black colored design.
History
* An exact date was found where he appeared in, otherwise, the edition release date is assumed.
Trivia
Akira is the 22nd character released in 2021 , after Frankette , Spacebot , Ox , Rat , Qin , Hou , Zayn , Darryl , Lana , Bonnie , Buzz , Trym , Pridebot , Pride Jake , Pride Fresh , Pride Spike , Pride Tricky , Pride Yutani , Pride Frank , Alexandre , and Parkour Rin .
He is the second surfer, with the first being Harumi and the third being Yuto .
He is the third Japanese character. The first being Yutani (half American), the second being Harumi , the fourth being Yuto , the fifth being Oni Hime , and the sixth being Saru .
Akira is the sixth racer surfer, the first being Zoe in her Biker Outfit, Roberto , Nick in his Speed Outfit, Philip in his Racer Outfit and Kareem .
He is the first surfer to have a real vehicle (motorcycle) in his animation.
He is the first surfer to have a real vehicle (motorcycle) in his animation.
He resembles Kaneda , the protagonist of the 1988 Japanese animated film " Akira ". His color scheme and motorbike are similar to Kaneda's.
He is the fifth character to wear a mask. The 1st being Ninja , the 2nd being Jaro , the 3rd being Miss Maia , the 4th being Zayn , the 6th being Pink , the 7th being Super Runner Tricky , the 8th being Pride Miss Maia , and the 9th being Thunder .
He is the first Olympic surfer, the others being Hélène , Leo , Sean Snow , and Mia .
For unknown reasons, he didn't get his first outfit in , but he still returned for .
Sakura and Override are his boards .
He is currently paywalled in all of his re-releases. However, SYBO claims that Akira may return for free in the future.
Diuring and , his Night Rider outfit was no longer paywalled, however his original outfit is still paywalled.
Diuring and , his Night Rider outfit was no longer paywalled, however his original outfit is still paywalled.
After four years, Akira finally gets his outfit in .
He is the second surfer to receive a new outfit in 2025 (Night Rider in ), after Song Yi .
He is the second surfer to receive a new outfit in 2025 (Night Rider in ), after Song Yi .
In the Chinese version, his name is Takeda (武田) and his face design is different.
In the 3.42.3 version, his Night Rider Outfit became limited.
His last appearance in the files was .
His zodiac sign is Cancer ♋️.
His birthday is between June 21 and July 22 (the exact date is unknown).
His birthday is between June 21 and July 22 (the exact date is unknown).

Alarm On
Alarm On is a limited board released in and Guard King ’s board. It has 3 abilities, two of them each cost 150. Super Jump is its default ability.
Appearance
It is a green frame with a warning sign as a platform and a flashlight taped to the front. When Super Speed is applied, it will give the board some grey exhaust pipes in the bottom back area of the surfboard. When Smooth Drift is applied, it will add a white and red ramp-shaped fin on the top-back part of the surfboard.
Trivia
It, 12th Birthday , Pixel Hoverboard and Street Grace are the 20th, 21st, 22nd and 23rd boards to released in 2024 after Skyrocket , Electro Shock , Bada-Bomb , Confetti Crush , Red Velvet , Roaring Heart , Lunar Scroll , Astro Tune , Interstellar , Shamrock Shebang , Megga Bite , Fast and Festive , Radiant Rider , Jak , Tea Timer , Deli-Vroom , Yellow Sub , Jelly Board , Koinobori , and Mayo Mania .
This is the first board to have 3 abilities, but it still has 2 upgrades, the second board being Heavy Metal , the third being Sci-Fly , the fourth being Cozy Cruiser , the fifth being Jingle Dingle , and the sixth being Heartstopper .
This is the second board to have a default ability with upgrades, the first is Street Grace , the third is Glarx 3000 , the fourth is Heavy Metal , the fifth is Arrow , and the sixth is Sci-Fly .
This board currently has the most expensive upgrades, costing a total of 300.
This is the 3rd board of 2024 that has a default ability, the 1st being Fast and Festive , the 2nd being Koinobori , the 4th being Street Grace , the 5th being Standing Ovation , the 6th being Glarx 3000 , the 7th being Heavy Metal , the 8th being Arrow , the 9th being Chop-Chop Board , the 10th being Sci-Fly , the 11th being Skeletuna , the 12th being Pathfinder , the 13th being Cozy Cruiser , the 14th being Suitcase Saga , the 15th being Jingle Dingle , and the 16th being Shiny Surprise .
It has the same abilities as Sci-Fly and Jingle Dingle .
In the Chinese Version, this board is 2024 board and called Patrol Light (巡逻灯) and it doesn't have Super Jump upgrade.
It is Guard King ’s board .

Alba
Alba  is a limited character released on . She can be purchased with 95,000. Both her 1st outfit, the Cowgirl Outfit and her 2nd outfit, the Space Outfit can be bought for 30, respectively.
Appearance
Default Outfit
Alba is an American teenage green alien girl from . She has blue eyes and brown hair with pink on the end with pink highlights. She wears a pink, white and blue tight-fitting suit, white gloves with blue rings on the end of them and white boots with blue rings on the top and pink ends. She also has pink lips and pink blush. She also has green antennas on her head.
Cowgirl Outfit
Launched in , she is a brown, long haired girl with light brown skin and a cowgirl hat. She wears blue earrings with brown/turquoise boots. She wears tan shorts with a brown, silver and turquoise belt. She wears a shirt with some sort of red pattern. She also wears blue bracelet on her right wrist, while a white and turquoise bracelet is on her left wrist. However, she isn't wearing pink lipstick.
Space Outfit
This outfit was first introduced in the trailer. In her Space Outfit, she now wears an orange astronaut suit, a blue belt with a star and blue-cream boots with stars.
Trivia

Alex
Alex is a character from the update. She is the eleventh limited character in Subway Surfers and the ninth limited character in the World Tour series. She costs  95,000, just like all other limited characters. Her Techno outfit costs 75.
Appearance
Default Outfit
Alex is a Russian teenage girl from who most likely must be a dancer because of her animation. She has black hair and grey eyes. She wears a ushanka, yellow overalls with black leggings as well as red boots with thick grey tokens.
Techno Outfit
In her Techno outfit (released in ), she wears a yellow beanie, a black varsity jacket with white lines on the sleeves, a red vest with a yellow container, black pants with flame designs on the bottom, red fingerless gloves, and white sneakers.
Trivia
One of her idles has Alex do the Hopak, a traditional Ukrainian folk dance.

Alexandre
Alexandre is a limited character released in update. He costs 15,000.
His Stan Outfit released in  originally costing Tier 30 of  Season Hunt, now 150.
Appearance
Default Outfit
Alexandre is a French teenage boy and street artist from , who has fair skin and hazel eyes. He has messy blonde hair and wears a petrol blue cap with gold features. He has a midnight green, teal, and gold colored jacket and underneath he wears a white t-shirt. He wears light beige shorts with gray spots. He has a light blue belt where it has some spray cans. On the left leg he wears a long black stocking while the right one wears a short stocking of the same color. He has white shoes with black laces. He wears gray and yellow headphones and has a band-aid on his left cheek.
Stan Outfit
Launched in , he has pinkish-black hair and a white-purple cap, his eyes have become more saturated and around them are pink shadows, on his right ear are 2 gold earrings. Under the white hoodie with "Stan 지영" (A Fan Ji-Yeong ) written on it, there is a gray T-shirt, black shorts, white socks with purple stripes, black sneakers with gray laces. This outfit is dedicated to Ji-Yeong . Stan is a slang term meaning superfan or enthusiast.

Alfie
Alfie is a limited character released in the  update. Alfie is the 162nd character in the game. His 1st outfit, the Cupid Outfit costs 400 in . Now it costs . His 2nd outfit, the Backyard Troll Outfit costs 400 in . Now it costs
Appearance
Default Outfit
Alfie is a 13-year-old teenage boy with black hair and bright green eyes from / . He wears a green hoodie with elf ears attached to it. He has a lemon yellow sweater with two brown belts going around it, and jeans with a belt as well. His shoes are red and white with a leaf attached to both of them. He carries a bow and arrow with him.
Cupid Outfit
Launched in , he now wears a red cap, along with it he has 2 angel ears, he wears a pastel red and white sweater, in addition to having a deep red shirt, he wears white pants, with blue socks, he wears red and white shoes In it there are 2 heart-shaped cords, he also uses angel wings on his back and finally the arrow becomes a heart.
Backyard Troll Outfit
Launched in , he wears a garter with two reindeer antlers attached to it. He wears a maroon shirt with blank drawing, he has gray pants, also he wears brown shoes with orange laces, and he has the same arrow of his default outfit.

Alia
Alia is a limited character from . She costs 210. Her first outfit, Sneaky Blinders Outfit costs 400 in , currently 150.
Appearance
Default Outfit
Alia is an Indian teenage girl from who has dark short hair, brown eyes, and dark eyebrows along a pierced nose. In her original outfit, she wears a yellow crop top with green detail, she wears green fingerless gloves with black edges, orange pants, and yellow shoes, and she also wears a bag on her back she has a flower on the top left of her head. In the trailer screenshot, her skin below her crop top appears to be yellow like her crop top.
Sneaky Blinders Outfit
Launched in , she now wears a light brown cap, she has a white shirt with a dark gray tie, she wears a dark brown vest, her back bag is dark brown, she wears khaki pants, she wears brown gloves, her shoes are black with light brown laces, has two black bracelets on both arms and a single white earring.

Alicia
Alicia is a limited character released in . She costs 60. She is the 53rd limited character in Subway Surfers and the 51st limited character in the World Tour. series. Her 1st outfit, the Tracksuit Outfit, costs 30. Her 2nd outfit, the Fall Outfit was released on and it was up for sale for 25,000. Now, it costs 50.
Appearance
Default Outfit
Alicia is a Latina-American teenage girl from who wears gold headphones, as well as a red jumper with black stripes and a white letter "A" on it. She wears a gold necklace and has white trousers with black stripes and black and gold shoes. She has long brown hair.
Tracksuit Outfit
Launched in , she has zomp hair with two buns, one on each side of her hair hanging down. She also has zomp eyebrows. She wears bright yellow-orange sunglasses with purple lipstick, a lighter red and blue tracksuit jumper showing her midriff and saying “Sub Surf”, matching tracksuit trousers and white sneakers.
Fall Outfit
Launched in , she wears a dark green coat, with the left sleeve in light blue and the right sleeve in orange and a dark yellow jacket on top. She wears wine red fall pants and brown winter boots. Her hair is longer and is tied with maple leaves.

Alma Brava
Alma Brava is a character released in . She is the 250th character in the game and the 35th surfer of 2025 .
Appearance
Default Outfit
Alma Brava is a 16-year-old Mexican teenage girl from who wears a sugar skull makeup in honor of Dia de los Muertos . She also has a red and green themed outfit: from her rose headband, a cropped shirt layered on long-sleeved undershirt with wide sleeves, and short skirt. Her footwear includes two black shoes with white crisscross laces and yellow-blue accents, which matches with her blue knee pads. She has a pair of large blue, circular earrings for accessories.

Amira
Amira was launched as a character in . She was also the 25th limited character in Subway Surfers , and 23rd limited character in the World Tour series. She can be purchased with 100,000, just like some characters. Both her 1st outfit, the Genie Outfit and 2nd outfit, the Jewel Outfit, can be bought for 75 each. From to , her default and first outfit were non-limited, but her second outfit was still limited.
Appearance
Default Outfit
Amira is an Arabian teenage girl from who has straight brown hair, fashioned uniquely in a side ponytail with bangs, adorned with a wide yellow headband. Her eyes are brown and her skin is moderately tanned. Amira's outfit includes a wheat colored tank top with dark forest green stripes that shows her belly button and bright magenta pants with a belt matching her top. She wears multiple gold accessories, which include a pair of wide armlets, a snake-shaped upper arm bracelet, a small hoop earring which is connected with a golden chain to a similar nose ring. Probably the most noticeable trait about her is that she is barefoot. Until her skin color texture was saturated.
Genie Outfit
Launched in , she has blue skin and black hair which sticks up much like the Genie from Aladdin . She now has a white cloth-like material around her head. She wears a light pink top and the same long magenta pants. She has a red shirt and shoes with the end of the shoe curving. Finally, she has a white material that has bumps all across the material. She loses the chain that came out of her nose and around her right earring. She also has blue eyes and pink lipstick.
Jewel Outfit
Launched in , she wears a striped blue and flesh colored dress, white tights, plum boots, gold bangles, a plum headscarf and a gold ring on her head with a big red jewel in the center. She gets back her nose piercing with a chain connecting it to her earring.
History
* An exact date was found where she appeared in, otherwise, the edition release date is assumed.
Trivia
Her Genie Outfit is a reference to the Genie from the film Aladdin . Not only is her skin blue like the Genie, but her hair is also styled the same way. She also wears the same yellow bracelets that the Genie wears.
She is the third character to run barefooted, the first being Izzy , the second being Jay (Blue Outfit and Color Master Outfit only) and the fourth being Zuri (Original Outfit only).
Amira (Almirah) means "Princess (الاميره)" in Arabic.
Even though her name means "Princess", she is not a royal character.
Even though her name means "Princess", she is not a royal character.

Among Us Tour
Among Us Tour is a limited collaboration event during the and updates, in collaboration with Among Us . is similar to this challenge. On the Among Us Tour, the player has to collect Report Tokens to win prizes including Emergency Meeting Boxes which can unlock all the six crewmate characters. These surfers are Crewmate Jake , Crewmate Tricky , Crewmate Spike , Crewmate King , Crewmate Tasha and Crewmate Guard . This event has 5 rounds.

Amy
Amy  is a limited character released in the update. She originally came from the Chinese version of the game. She debuted in the Western version of the game with a purchase with real money in a Bundle with Spaceship . She returned in for .
Her 1st outfit, the Panda Outfit, now costs 75.
Appearance
Default Outfit
Amy is a Chinese-American teenage girl from / , who wears a blue and white space suit with black shoes. A small piece of her dark brown hair can be seen resting on her forehead.
Panda Outfit
Launched in / , she wears more casual clothes with a panda theme, a blue tiara with panda ears, golden earrings and red lipstick. She also wears a white t-shirt with dark blue sleeves with a panda face print, yellow shorts, green socks and yellow boots with blue and brown details
History

Andy
Andy is a limited character from . He can be unlocked with 75 . His first outfit, the Gamer Outfit, can be unlocked for 75 . His second outfit, The Great Outdoors Outfit, could initially be unlocked by completing 5 stages of the Play 2 Plant event during (later 75 ).
Appearance
Default Outfit
Andy is an American teenage dark-skinned boy from with teal hair tied up in a bun. He has brown eyes and wears red glasses. He wears a plaid red shirt over a black T-shirt, short jeans with white stripes, teal sneakers with white laces, dark blue socks, a gray bracelet on his right wrist, and a metallic watch that emits a green glow on his left wrist. He also carries a black laptop.
Gamer Outfit
Launched in , he wears a white virtual reality headset and headphones. He wears a black hoodie with white stripes and red trim, a belt with various pouches, red shorts, black leggings, and white shoes with yellow laces. He wears a red glove on his left hand and a gaming glove (possibly inspired by Nintendo's "power glove" accessory) on his right.
The Great Outdoors Outfit
Launched in , his hair is brown and his skin has a different tone. He wears a plaid brown shirt over a white T-shirt, gray boots with red laces, a brown backpack, and black shorts with red stripes.
History
* An exact date was found where he appeared in, otherwise, the edition release date is assumed.
Trivia
Andy is the 16th new character from 2020 , following Liu , Ming , Monkbot , Callum , Jack , Dak , Lamar , Tom , Odell , Patrick , Saquon , Tankbot , Cathy , Morgan , and Noel .
Andy's animations reference Blades of Brim , another game created by SYBO Games .
In the animation for Andy's Original Outfit, Andy goes into a platformer video game with coins from Blades of Brim.
In the animation for Andy's Gamer Outfit, Andy goes into a Blades of Brim game where he is on a bridge shooting Goons, the most common enemies in Blades of Brim.

Arcade Game
Subway Surfers Arcade Game is an arcade version of the Subway Surfers that you can play in a machine in a room, and it exists in some malls in Denmark, Argentina, the United Arab Emirates and Bulgaria.

Arena Rider
Arena Rider is a hoverboard released in . Like any other hoverboard, it protects the character from crashing for 30 seconds. When crashed, it will need to recharge for a few seconds before it can be used again.
Appearance
This hoverboard has a red and blue shield-inspired base with silver frames and wings. The front has a brown extension which resembles a hand forming a fist, and a rear derived from a gladiator helmet's red crest.

Ash
Ash (the full/real name is Ash Rutherford ) is a character exclusive to Subway Surfers City . They could be unlocked by a in-app purchase offer inside the shop, or Seasons 7 Subway Pass. Their unlock able method in the Global Release is TBA.
Appearance
The Hot-headed Pitcher
Ash is obsessed with baseball and dreams of becoming the most celebrated baseball player of all time - but no matter how much they try, they always seem to come up short on tryouts due to poor batting performance and fiery temper tantrums.
Power-ups
Bat The Cache
Get bonus score from stomping on buried caches.
Heavy Striker
Chance of getting a random power-up by stomping on bouncy tarps.
Hitting Streak
Get a bonus score multiplier of 0.1 for each buried cache stomped (max 2.0).
Trivia
Their hoverboard is Home Runner.
Ash is the 17th character in Subway Surfers City.
They are the 7th character exclusive to Subway Surfers City. With the first being Rosalita, second being Billy, third being Jay, fourth being Lilah, fifth being Zara and sixth being Jaewoo.
Ash is most likely the first non-binary character in Subway Surfers City.
In earlier versions of Subway Surfers City, their hoverboard could be found in the files as “Nicholas Board”, which indicates that their name were previously Nicholas and later changed to Ash .

Asher
Asher is a character released during . He is the 237th character and the 22nd surfer of 2025 .
Appearance
Default Outfit
Asher is a 15-year-old Black American teenage boy surfer from who wears a black varsity jacket with a multicolored hoodie and sleeves, with the back side states the number eight (8). His orange and purple vest is matched with a white turtleneck underneath, as well as his light blue pants and brown outdoor shoes. There is also a red line painted on his left cheek.
Splasher Outfit
Released in , Asher now wears all-white shoes and overalls splashed with blue, purple, and green paint. He has a green buzzcut and dreads that have pink, blue, green, and red tips.

Astronave
Astronave is a limited hoverboard in Subway Surfers . It costs 400. Like any other board, it protects your character from crashing for 30 seconds.
Appearance
This hoverboard has the look of a spacecraft, hence its name "Astronave", which translates from Spanish as "Spacecraft" or "Spaceship". This board also has a blue effect coming from its propellants, imitating the effect that spaceships produce at the time of their departure from the ground.
Trivia
It is the third board of 2022 after Atomic Tile , and Lunar Tiger .
It is the third board, the first is Mosaic , the second is Stallion , the fourth is Fan-tastic , and the fifth, sixth and seventh are Float-Booster , Glitter Blaster , and Feeling Beachy .
It has the Super Speed and Zap Sideways powerups in the Chinese Version.
It didn't return in 2023 for unknown reasons.

In , it returned in the Flash Deals for 50,000.

In the Chinese Version, it's called Astrolabe (星盘) , and it have Zap Sideways and Double Jump upgrades.

This hoverboard has the second longest name out of all hoverboards. (15 characters long). Being the first Mean Green Machine (16 characters long)
This hoverboard is a reference to the Mattel hoverboard seen in 1989's movie Back to the Future Part II .
It's slightly different from it, since it couldn't use Mattel's logo because it's copyrighted by the real company. Other than that, it is an almost perfect replica.

Background Music
The Subway Surfers background music is the theme/soundtrack that plays in background while running as any character . The first soundtrack was different than the Main Theme, but it was still similar. The first soundtrack was the Soundtrack. The first time a fully remixed music was released was in (and was reused in ). The special remixed parts of these soundtracks were short (0:00-0:14), (0:56-1:04), until the update was released, in which every soundtrack began to be fully remixed from that edition onward. Every theme lasts around 2 minutes and 18 seconds (some soundtracks lasts 2 minutes and 20 seconds or more) and then loops.
List of Subway Surfers Background Musics
Trivia
The remix was the most altered version of the song (from a rock track to an EDM track).
The soundtrack is the first theme with vocals, is 2nd, is 3rd, (in 0:48 - 0:56) is 4th, the 5th is , the 6th is , the 7th is , the 8th is , the 9th is , the 10th is , the 11th is , the 12th is , the 13th is , the 14th is , the 15th is all soundtracks, and the 16th is .
Some editions (I.e or ) had remixes but they were only in the trailers for that edition the original music was used in-game instead.
is the 1st remix to be used in other editions ( and as a full remix).
is the first remix to be entirely sung by a voice.
In the game files of old versions the Subway Surfers theme is called New_Funky.mp3.

Bada-Bomb
Bada-Bomb is a hoverboard released in . Like any other board, it protects you from crashing for 30 seconds.
Appearance
The Bada-Bomb board has the shape of a missile launcher (per its name), with it being wider at the back. It has little "ventilators" on the side. On the front, it has blue flames. The whole board has a color pattern of: blue, gray and red.
Trivia
This is the third hoverboard of 2024 , after Skyrocket and Electro Shock .
This is the fourth board after Moped , Skyrocket , and Electro Shock .
It has the same colors as Super Runner Fernando , Super Runner Jake , Super Surfer , and Zuper Zapper .
In and , this board came back in the Daily Calendar again (Only to the people that already had Viperfish and Neo-Seoul in the past).
In , this board came back in the Season Hunt .
It is the first board from 2024 that has no abilities, the second being Confetti Crush , the third being Red Velvet , the fourth being Lunar Scroll , the fifth being Radiant Rider , the sixth being 12th Birthday , the seventh being Sporty Summer , the eighth being Moon Cake , and the ninth being Flash of Flavor .
In the Chinese Version, it's (Shenzhen 2025) board, and it's called Blue Flame (蓝色火焰) and it have Zap Sideways upgrade.

Badges
Badges is a feature released in version 3.49.1 of the update. It shares similarities with achievements and is part of the Player Profile .
When unlocking a badge, the player would get 3 and be able to display it in their Player Profile . For each rank unlocked in a certain badge, the player will receive additional amount of keys and the color of their badge will change according to the rank they are currently in:
Bronze (default; bronze, +3 )
Silver (silver, +3 )
Gold (gold, +4 )
Diamond (blue-purple, +6 )
Champion (multicolored, +10 )
Common Badges
These are badges that are always attainable in any season:
Special/Unique Badges
These are badges that are either only available during a specific season, or does not undergo a ranking system:
Trivia
You can only show up to four badges in your Player Profile .
The Badge rankings are the same as Top Run rankings.
Badges can be upgraded up to five times unlike Achievements, which can only be upgraded up to four times.
Badges can be upgraded up to five times unlike Achievements, which can only be upgraded up to four times.
According to one of the tips shown in the Loading Screens , a Birthday badge can be unlocked every May.
This feature is not available in Subway Surfers + .

Ballerina Latticina
Ballerina Latticina is a character released in . She is the 262nd character in the game and the 47th surfer of 2025 .
Appearance
Default Outfit
Ballerina Latticina is an 18-year-old young woman/teenager ballerina that has a cup of latte with a face for a head. She wears a full pastel pink ballerina costume: from her top, tutu, stockings, armbands, and sneakers.
Trivia
She, Mr. Gobbles and Rosie are the 45th, 46th and 47th surfers released in 2025 , after Snake , Tang Yuan , Valentina , Kaleo , Rebeca Roar , Gabriel , Dinorange , Rocky , Rani , Finlay , Ribbon , Chicken Jake , Mallard Tricky , Oni Hime , Saru , LOC , Stevie , Berta , Costa , Yutani Tree Frog , Rochelle Rebelle , Asher , White Rabbit , Peter , Tara , Henry , Sunny , 8 Ball Jake , Piper , El Primo , Spike , Dynamike , Poco , Max , Alma Brava , Mateo , Crewmate Jake , Crewmate Tricky , Crewmate Spike , Crewmate King , Crewmate Tasha , Crewmate Guard , Joo Won , and Si Woo .
She is the eighth surfer, the first being Tony , the second being Darryl , the third being Lana , the fourth being Rain , the fifth being Berta , the sixth being Mr. Gobbles and the seventh being Rosie .
She is the second Ballerina character in the game, the first being Ballerina Tricky .
She is based on an AI brainrot character called " Ballerina Cappuccina ."
She reuses Amy 's animations.
She is the 46th surfer to reuse animations after Frankette , J Balvin , Vivienne , Captain Flash , Clockwork Johnny , Thunder , Azura , Astra , Lucky , Red Panda , Faiza , Electra , Riley , Glorgalax , Ventura , Leo , Ozzy , Billy Bean , Kedi , Ruhi , Pumpkin Pete , Leif , Demi Lovato , Kaleo , Rani , Finlay , Stevie , Berta , Costa , Rochelle Rebelle , Asher , White Rabbit , Peter , Henry , Tara , Sunny , Alma Brava , Mateo , Crewmate Jake , Crewmate Tricky , Crewmate Spike , Crewmate King , Crewmate Tasha , Crewmate Guard and Rosie .

Battle of the Blizzard is an official competition.

Elf Jake
Elf Fresh
Elf Yutani
The Red Festive Team includes
Festive Frank
Festive Tricky
Festive Spike

First Round : Elf Jake vs Festive Spike
Second Round : Elf Fresh vs Festive Tricky
Third Round : Elf Yutani vs Festive Frank
Winners : Red Festive Team: Festive Frank Festive Tricky Festive Spike

Berta
Berta is a character released in update. She is the 223rd character in the game and the 18th surfer of 2025 . She is the winner of the Glitter and Glam Craft-a-Character Contest, made by @aisberg_aaa .
Appearance
Default Outfit
Berta is a 17-year-old Castillan/Spanish genderfluid teenage person from (originally from ) who loves wearing flashy, neon-colored clothes. She has a side-swept undercut hair in different colors, with a bright pink-blue-yellow facepaint and suit paired in a red skirt. Her purple boots have pink heels and three sparkle doodles in blue and yellow outlines. On top of that, her nails are also in assorted colors, and her big sparkle earrings is on the right ear to match with her yellow eyebrow and ear piercings.
Summer Eleganza Outfit
Launched in , Berta now wears a pink and green sundress wrapped with red and yellow fabric on the waist area, in addition to her green sandals with red heels and her usual yellow sparkle design. She also wears a big floppy sun hat, golden sunglasses with red lenses, as well as pink and yellow bracelets.

Big Kahuna
Big Kahuna is a non-limited hoverboard in Subway Surfers released in Subway Surfers: Holiday . Like any other board, it protects you from crashing for 30 seconds. When crashed, the board will explode and will need a few seconds to recharge to be used again.
During the , it received a new board upgrade, called Stay Low, which costs 60.
During the , it received a new board upgrade, called Smooth Drift, which costs 55.
Appearance
It has the appearance of a typical orange and white surfboard. The Stay Low upgrade adds two blue handles to the sides and a red triangle to the front. The Smooth Drift upgrade adds two green gliding wings to the bottom.
Trivia
When using this board, the character surfing on it will act like a surfer on the ocean.
Big Kahuna, Wave Rider , and Retro Wave are the only surfboards in the game as of .
Unlike the others, this is a non-limited hoverboard.
Unlike the others, this is a non-limited hoverboard.
Big Kahuna is the first hoverboard to receive the Stay Low ability as an upgrade. The second board is the Lumberjack .
It has the same upgrades as Interstellar .
In the Chinese Version, it's a board, and it's called Surfboard (滑浪板) .

Billy
Billy is a character exclusive to Subway Surfers City .
Appearance
Billy has long, black hair and blue eyes with dark circles. He wears a black, tattered, stained shirt with a white skull on the chest, green shorts with a black belt with a silver buckle, and silver chain, and worn, red-and-white sneakers with white, red and blue socks. He wears a red sweatband, rings on both hands, black armbands- the one on the left being adorned with spikes- and what appears to be a glucose monitor on his right arm. He holds brown drumsticks in both hands.

Billy Bean
Billy Bean is a character released in . He is the 204th character in the game.
Appearance
Default Outfit
Billy Bean is a 13-year-old Australian teenage burger boy from . He has a burger head with two midnight green eyes, a green striped shirt, maroon shorts with red cuffs, and white and orange shoes. He also has bread like skin on his arms and legs.
History
* An exact date was found where he appeared in, otherwise, the edition release date is assumed.

Birthday
Birthday is a holiday that is celebrated in the Subway Surfers World Tour updates on May 24 .
Info
Info
Birthday special World Tour editions
Trivia
In 2022 , there was an special event dedicated to the game's 10th Birthday; the Super Runners , which features 5 superhero variants of the Core Crew; Super Runner Jake , Super Runner Tricky , Super Runner Fresh , Super Runner Yutani , and Super Runner Fernando , along with their boards; Super Surfer , Trickster , Boombastic , Mag-Neat-O , and Zuper Zapper .
In 2023 , there was an special event dedicated to the game's 11th Birthday; Rivals , which features 4 rivals of the Core Crew; Catrine , Bruno , Hali , and Lawrence , along with their boards; Danger Dasher , Future Wave , Rave Rider , and Sharpeed .
Starting with 2021 , the game's birthday is always celebrated in SYBO 's hometown; in .
However, in 2024 , it was celebrated in instead, making this the only year of the 2020 's decade to not have a birthday update celebrated in .
However, in 2024 , it was celebrated in instead, making this the only year of the 2020 's decade to not have a birthday update celebrated in .

Birthday Comeback
Birthday Comeback  was an event that first occurred during the edition. It lasted for 21 days. Every 3 days, returning characters will be offered a multiplier bonus.
3 Years later, in May 7th 2020 , the update, that celebrated the game's 8 year anniversary with a new look for the whole game, had a special Birthday Comeback for the game's 8 years from 2012 to 2020 . Each promotion had two unique characters consecutive year and the 8th Birthday board for free on all offers.
In , a new Birthday Bonanza was released and has been used for and . The sales would refresh every week with new characters and/or different costs. Each free character would cost 75, 99, or 99,000. Every other character would cost .
In , a new event named 200th World Tour ( ) was released and is a limited world map which reused 21 destinations and included several rewards which can be claimed after collecting 10-20 super suitcases from each map. The following locations in sequential order are , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , and .

The Birthday Frame is a frame released in the update. This frame could be unlocked in the One Run Challenge event in during .
Appearance

Trivia
This was released in the update.
You would unlock this frame by getting to the final tier of the One Run Challenge event in .
This is the second frame to be released, after the Default Frame in for Top Run and for Player Profile .
It might be a reference to the old V1 menu UIs which looked like a notebook with drawings like in this frame.

Bjarki
Bjarki  is a limited character released in update. He is the thirty-fifth limited character in Subway Surfers and the thirty-third limited character in the World Tour series . Like all other limited characters, he can be unlocked for 95,000. Both his first outfit, the Power Outfit, and second outfit, the Fisher Outfit, can be bought for 75 each.
Appearance
Default Outfit
Bjarki is an Icelandic teenage boy from with red hair. He wears a gray sweater, brown pants, pink boots, a purple and blue scarf, and a red cap. He carries a sheep in his left arm.
Power Outfit
Launched in , he wears a dark blue tank top with white lines and a red Viking helmet print in the middle, a big brown belt, red shorts, white socks and green sneakers with yellow details. He also has black gloves on his hands and he still carries his sheep in his left arm.
Fisher Outfit
Launched in , he wears black boots, brown pants, a green sweater, a yellow jacket, and a gray cap. He has a fishing pole in his hand.
Trivia
He is the sixth new character of 2016 , the first being  Jenny , the second being Jaro , the third being  Aina , the fourth being  Carlos and the fifth being Jia .
He is the third male new character of 2016 , the first being  Jaro  and the second being Carlos .
He is the first red-headed character in the game, others being Lawrence , Rudy Rascal , and Audrey .
His Power Outfit might be based on Icelandic weightlifters and strongmen.
His price was decreased from 95,000 to 5,000 during the World Tour .
Every other sheep in the game looks very similar to the sheep that Bjarki is always holding.
He returns for Wild Wednesdays in , , , , and .
He gets +5 Multiplier Bonus for Special Thursdays in , , and .

Black Friday Events
Black Friday is the Friday after Thanksgiving in the United States. It traditionally marks the start of the Christmas shopping season in the United States. Many stores offer highly promoted sales at discounted prices and often open early, sometimes as early as midnight or even on Thanksgiving. Some stores' sales continue to Monday ("Cyber Monday") or for a week ("Cyber Week"). In this case, SYBO Games has limited offers that are sometimes cheaper than what you would usually spend in the game. The characters that cost usually cost between $2.99-$9.99 (USD) and some characters would either cost 75, 99, or 99,000. The first bundle for Black Friday was released during . Then, they used character sales/bonanzas in , , , , and . However, the number of paywalls has increased each year. In , the character bonanza was called "The Best Deal of the year!", although this sale is meant for Black Friday. The Black Friday Super Pack was renamed to Rockin' Rebels In . All of these sales are available from Black Friday to Cyber Monday. Here is a list of what was available during each season and the cost of each item.
Seattle

Bling
BLING is a song by Ji-Yeong , one of the soundtracks of . It was released in , on November 1st, 2023 . It's available in the game's "boombox" as background music and also in music streaming services, like Spotify or Apple Music. SEOUL soundtrack is based on this song, but there is almost no lyrics here.
https://subwaysurf.fandom.com/wiki/File:Subway-Surfers-Seoul-2023-Bling-Soundtrack.mp3

Credits
Trivia
This is the first song in the game (all previous soundtracks were instrumental) with Higher (in ), Surf as Yourself (in ), Vamos Dançar (in ), Colors (in ), I'll Always Win (in ) and Energize (in ) coming after.
The song's genre is K-pop, which is very popular in South Korea.
This is the debut of Ji-Yeong as a singer, she later performed Higher together with Astra and Stella in the "Supernovas" group.
In real life, the vocals for this song were performed by a guest artist, Zhavanya Meidi .

Blob Frame
The Blob Frame is a frame released in the update. This frame could be unlocked in the One Run Challenge event in during .
Appearance
A frame in different shades of green that shows blobs dripping down from it.
Trivia
This is the 24th frame to be released. The previous frames are the Default Frame in for Top Run and for Player Profile , the Birthday Frame in , the Hollywood Frame in , the Summer Frame in , the Plant and Lava Frames in , the Club Frame in , the Pride Frame in , the Oxford Green and Oxford Red Frames in , the Brawl Stars and Gems Frames in , the Pumpkin and Dia de los Muertos Frames in , the Candy Cane and Gingerbread Frames in , the Glorx and Cinema Frames in , the Winter Frame in , the Shamrock Frame in , the Easter Egg Frame in , and the Earth Day and Koi Frames in .
This is the eighth frame released in 2026 , after Glorx Frame , Cinema Frame , Winter Frame , Shamrock Frame , Easter Egg Frame , Earth Day Frame , and Koi Frame .

Bloom Boom
Bloom Boom is a hoverboard released in . Like any other hoverboard, it protects the character from crashing for 30 seconds. When crashed, it will need to recharge for a few seconds before it can be used again.
Appearance
A dark green hoverboard that is shaped like the original Hoverboard but is seemingly wrapped in a lighter green "leaf" or "clothing." The rear sides feature a yellow flower with an elongated leaf.
Trivia
It, Temple Rush , and Firefly5 are the 23rd, 24th, and 25th boards released in 2026 . The other 2026 boards are Road Rage , Frankmobile , Perfectoplasma , Fire Starter , Xtra Cool , Turbo Love , Gold Rush , The Green Flame , Turbo Love , Marbleous , Arena Rider , Major Speed , Sunny Side Up , Pixel Perfect , Buzz BNY , Fast Fur-ward , Cathletic Ride , Fox Trotter , Sewer Surfer , Rat Trap , 14th Birthday , and Bomb-Bastic .
It is the fifth board, after Naga , Coral , Temple Rush , and Firefly5 .
It is Lestari 's second board . Her first board is Firefly5 .

Bloomster
Bloomster is a hoverboard released in . Like any other hoverboard, it protects the character from crashing for 30 seconds. When crashed, it will need to recharge for a few seconds before it can be used again.
Appearance
Trivia
It, Eggscalibur , Edinburst , and Guard Board are the 13th, 14th, 15th, and 16th boards released in 2025 , after Hyper Viper , Lantern , Override , Fresh Love , Heartstopper , Dear Dart , Roarsome , Arara Board , Moongazer , Purple Spin , Cyan Note , and Blue Riff .
It is the fourth board, the first being Punk , the second being I-Scream , and the third being Eggscalibur .
It is Ribbon 's board .
It has same upgrades as Monster , Smoking Slime , Jazz , Sharpeed , Yellow Sub , Mayo Mania , Veggie Velocity , The De-lyre-ious , and Bias Boom .
In , it costs in Offer Trails .
In , it came back in the One Run Challenge in .

Blue Ice
Blue Ice is a non-limited hoverboard , released in . Like any other hoverboard, it protects you from crashing for 30 seconds. When crashed, the board will explode and recharge for a few seconds before it can be used again. It costs 70.
Appearance
It has a shape of a self-balancing scooter with cyan, and green designs on it.
Trivia
Blue Ice is one of the 5 non-limited hoverboards launched in , the others being Crew , Surf & Spray , Crew Crush and Wild Style .
It and Crew Crush are based on Subway Surfers themed "hoverboards" by Voyager you can buy in real life at Walmart or Target.
Its name is shared with Jay 's Blue outfit (not counting Ice ) and Elf Tricky 's Ice outfit (not counting Blue ).
In , it can be unlocked in a bundle along with Buzz which costs 180,000 in total
In , it can be unlocked in the same bundle with Buzz , but it costs 350,000 instead.
This and Crew Crush are the only non-limited hoverboards to cost .

Blue Riff
Blue Riff is a hoverboard released in . Like any other hoverboard, it protects the character from crashing for 30 seconds. When crashed, it will need to recharge for a few seconds before it can be used again.
Appearance
Is a colorful hoverboard that resembles a futuristic gadget, featuring a circular blue top, a long golden body with purple accents, and three orange sections at the bottom. The design has a unique shape and bold colors.
Trivia
It, Purple Spin , and Cyan Note are the 10th, 11th, and 12th boards released in 2025 , after Hyper Viper , Lantern , Override , Fresh Love , Heartstopper , Dear Dart , Roarsome , Arara Board , and Moongazer .
It is the seventh board, the first being Bengal , the second being Color Cloud , the third being Beach , the fourth being Aero Fish , the fifth being Purple Spin , and the sixth being Cyan Note .
It is also the second 2025 board to have no ability, the first is Moongazer .
It's Stella 's second board. Her first board is Interstellar .

Board Upgrades
Board Upgrades are special powers given to  hoverboards . Some normal hoverboards have their own special ability(s). All board upgrades, except the Star Trail (which is free) cost an amount between 30 and 60. They were added into the game during to be given for  Superhero  and  Starboard . And every edition after that, board upgrades are added for other hoverboards like  Monster , Big Kahuna and Great White . The table below shows the board upgrades each hoverboard has.
Types of Upgrades
NOTE: PRICES OF THE UPGRADES MAY BE OUT OF DATE.
The types of upgrades are as follows:
Speed Up/Super Speed : 30-60 keys. Available on Great White , Monster , Scoot , Color Cloud , Rocket , 4th of July , Jazz , Aero , Crash Test , Aero Fish , Smoking Slime , Big Boss Board , Future Wave , Sharpeed , Skyrocket , Roaring Heart , Astro Tune , Megga Bite , Jak , Tea Timer , Jelly Board , Yellow Sub , Mayo Mania , Alarm On , Rock n' Riley , Standing Ovation , Risky Ride , Heavy Metal , Arrow , Turbo Stump , Veggie Velocity , Sci-Fly , Skeletuna , Pitch Dark , Pathfinder , Cozy Cruiser , Jingle Dingle , Hyper Viper , Fresh Love , Heartstopper , Roarsome , Bloomster , Guard Board , Mizu Dash , You Are Here , Float-Booster , Roll & Race , Business Time , Principal's Nightmare , The De-lyre-ious , Fiesta , Bias Boom , Gobble Trouble , Candy Crash , Perfectoplasma , Fire Starter , Lucky Fish , Marbleous , Buzz BNY , Fast Fur-ward , Off-Road Ranger , Sweet Elite
Double Jump : 45-60 keys. Available on Starboard , Freestyler , Lumberjack , Danger Dasher , Future Wave , Rave Rider , Skyrocket , Electro Shock , Roaring Heart , Astro Tune , Deli-Vroom , Street Grace , Laser Flip , Champ , Brass Whizz , Cook-Express , Couch Surfer , Fear Tractor , Fall Speed , Suitcase Saga , Gadget Force , Lantern , Arara Board , Eggscalibur , You Are Here , Rabbit Racer , Moon Biter , Motor Mania , Shin Supreme , Pie Express , Turbo Love , The Green Flame , Marbleous , Pixel Perfect , Temple Rush , Bay Drifter
Zap Sideways : 40-60 keys. Available on Superhero , Phantom , Scoot , Tumi , Superstar
Super Jump : 30-60 keys. Available on Monster , Freestyler , Superhero , Bass Blaster , Magic Carpet , Stallion , Jazz , Maple Leaf , Smoking Slime , Flaming Doll , Superstar , Sharpeed , Yellow Sub , Mayo Mania , Sunny Sailor , Heavy Metal , Raggedy Runner , Veggie Velocity , Catalyst , Sci-Fly , Fox Trail , Cozy Cruiser , Jingle Dingle , Shiny Surprise , Heartstopper , Roarsome , Purple Spin , Bloomster , Trespasser , Glitter Blaster , Street Style , Brainstormer , The De-lyre-ious , Finish Line , Bias Boom , Mean Green Machine , Frankmobile , Fire Starter , Arena Rider , Fast Fur-ward , Sewer Surfer , Rat Trap , Off-Road Ranger , Yellow Fellow
Stay Low : 50-60 keys. Available on Big Kahuna , Lumberjack , Danger Dasher , Flaming Doll , Interstellar , Megga Bite , Pixel Hoverboard , Risky Ride , Heavy Metal , Chop-Chop Board , Cozy Cruiser , Override , Arara Board , Purple Spin , Seven Seas , Street Style , Business Time , Motor Mania , Charmer , Temple Rush
Smooth Drift : 30-60 keys. Available on Great White , Sunset , Orni , Wayfarer , Magic Carpet , Aero , Maple Leaf , Color Shift , Jamanta , Rave Rider , Electro Shock , Interstellar , Shamrock Shebang , Jak , Tea Timer , Alarm On , Sunny Sailor , Glarx 3000 , Champ , Arrow , Raggedy Runner , Brass Whizz , Cook-Express , Couch Surfer , Fear Tractor , Sci-Fly , Fox Trail , Icicle , Jingle Dingle , Lantern , Heartstopper , Dear Dart , Roarsome , Cyan Note , Guard Board , Trespasser , Float-Booster , Brainstormer , Fiesta , Gobble Trouble , Mean Green Machine , Frankmobile , The Green Flame , Pixel Perfect , Fox Trotter , Sewer Surfer , Firefly5 , Sweet Elite
Pink Trail : 30 keys. Available on Sunset
Feather Trail : 30 keys. Available on Chicky
Snow Trail: 30 keys. Available on Jingles
Flower Trail: 30 keys. Available on Groovy
Dust Trail : 30 keys. Available on Old Dusty
Bubble Trail : 30 keys. Available on Turtle
Star Trail : Free. Available on Starboard
Music Trail : 30 keys. Available on Bass Blaster
Spark Trail : 30 keys. Available on Rocket , 4th of July
Horseshoe Trail : 30 keys. Available on Stallion
Color Splash : 40 keys. Available on Color Cloud
List of Upgraded Boards (Non-Limited)
They were given to non-limited hoverboards until the update.
List of Upgraded Boards (Limited)
Starting from the edition, limited hoverboards were given upgrades too.
History
Board Upgrades were added to the game on 28/8/2014 with the update. The first upgrades were: Star Trail for Starboard , and Zap Sideways for Superhero .
On 18/9/2014, the update added two new upgrades: Speed Up for Great White and Super Jump for Superhero .
On 9/10/2014, the added two new upgrades: Stay Low for Big Kahuna and Double Jump for Starboard .
On 6/11/2014, the update added two new upgrades: Smooth Drift for Great White and Speed Up for Monster .
On 4/12/2014, the update added two new upgrades: Smooth Drift for Big Kahuna and Double Jump for Freestyler .
On 1/1/2015, the update added two new upgrades: Pink Trail for Miami and Super Jump for Monster .
On 22/1/2015, the update added two new upgrades: Double Jump for Skull Fire and Super Jump for Freestyler .
On 12/2/2015, the update added two new upgrades: Smooth Drift for Miami and Zap Sideways for Scoot .
On 5/3/2015, the update added two new upgrades: Speed Up for Skull Fire and Double Jump for Lumberjack .
On 26/3/2015, the update added three new upgrades: Speed Up for Skull Fire , Stay Low for Lumberjack and Speed Up for Scoot .
On 3/12/2015, the update added two new upgrades: Snow Trail & Super Jump for Jingles .
On 10/3/2016, the update added two new upgrades: Feather Trail (misnamed Egg Trail) & Speed Up for Chicky .
On 5/7/2016, the update added one new upgrade: Smooth Drift for Orni .
On 13/10/2016, the update added one new upgrade: Zap Sideways for Phantom .
On 19/1/2017, the update added one new upgrade: Flower Trail for Groovy .
On 9/2/2017, the update added one new upgrade: Dust Trail for Old Dusty .
On 2/3/2017, the update added one new upgrade: Bubble Trail for Turtle .
On 28/12/2017, the update added two new upgrades: Speed Up & Smooth Drift for Scarab .
On 21/06/2018, the update added four new upgrades: Speed Up & Color Splash for Color Cloud and Music Trail & Super Jump for Bass Blaster .
On 20/9/2018, the update added a new upgrade: Smooth Drift for Wayfarer .
On 1/11/2018, the update added two new upgrades: Smooth Drift & Super Jump for Magic Carpet .
On 28/12/2018, the update added two new upgrades: Spark Trail & Speed Up for Rocket .
On 27/06/2019, the update added two new upgrades: Spark Trail & Speed Up for 4th of July .
On 15/8/2019, the update added two new upgrades: Horseshoe Trail & Super Jump for Stallion .
On 30/12/2019, the update added two new upgrades: Speed Up & Super Jump for Jazz .
On 23/1/2020, the update added two new upgrades: Speed Up & Smooth Drift for Aero .
On 7/5/2020, in the update, the boards upgrades price is changed. Those with 60 go down to 30, those from 80 to 40, those from 90 to 45, those from 100 to 50 and those from 110 to 60.
On 1/10/2020, the update added a new upgrade: Zap Sideways for Tumi .
On 1/11/2021, the update added two new upgrades: Smooth Drift and Super Jump for Maple Leaf .
On 25/4/2022, the update added a new upgrade: Super Speed for Crash Test .
On 19/09/2022, the update added a new upgrade: Super Speed for Aero Fish .
On 10/10/2022, the update added two new upgrades: Super Speed and Super Jump for Smoking Slime .
On 01/11/2022, the update added a new upgrade: Super Speed for Night Lute .
On 15/05/2023, the update added two new upgrades: Stay Low and Double Jump for Danger Dasher .
On 05/06/2023, the update added a new upgrade: Smooth Drift for Color Shift .
On 27/06/2023, the update added a new upgrade: Super Speed for Big Boss Board .
On 17/07/2023, the update added two new upgrades: Super Speed and Double Jump for Future Wave .
On 08/08/2023, the update added a new upgrade: Smooth Drift for Jamanta .
On 18/09/2023, the update added two new upgrades: Double Jump and Smooth Drift for Rave Rider .
On 09/10/2023, the update added two new upgrades: Stay Low and Super Jump for Flaming Doll .
