Super Bubble
Super Bubble is a power-up that was introduced in  on 18 January 2024 in the 3.23.1 update. It can be equipped via watching an ad to get the power up for 30 seconds long, or 2 ads to get the power up for 90 seconds long. It can protect the player while active and stores 200 points every 3 seconds (score can be multiplied with the Score Multiplier ). When finished (or if the player crashes before the timer expires), the stored score will be earned. Like the Score Booster , the player cannot use the Super Bubble again until a new run is started. This power-up is available in most event challenges like the Marathon Challenge , Tag Time Attack , Mystery Hurdles , Plant Invasion: No Floor Challenge and Floor Is Lava: No Floor Challenge .
In , it was removed from some users for unknown reasons. This issue was supposed to be resolved in but it was still not available to anyone who lost the power-up in , but was available to everyone again in . Players can use this 2x if they get it in the beginning of the run and appears again if the icon pops up.
Appearance
The Super Bubble takes on the appearance of a transparent bubble shield. The icon is a bubble with a star inside.
History
Trivia
It is one of the three power-ups obtainable by ads, the others being Multiplier Bonus and Score Blast .
It was not announced until but it was released in .
For unknown reasons, Super Bubble was not available in Classic Mode that was introduced in until . From , Super Bubble is finally available to use in Classic Mode.
However, in , Super Bubble is not available again in Classic Mode and Marathon Challenge
However, in , Super Bubble is not available again in Classic Mode and Marathon Challenge
For unknown reasons, it is not available every time in each event. For some players, Super Bubble is never available in events.
It is the tenth power-up in-game.
The Super Bubble makes a strange humming sound if the sound effects are not muted in the game.
Gallery
Navigation

Super Jackpots
Super Jackpots are EXTREMELY rare at a 0.08% chance but yield 300,000 . It is the rarest prize players can earn from the Super Mystery Box . Super Mystery Boxes are generally earned after completing missions , collecting all the letters in the Word Hunt , completing the Weekly Hunt , or collecting them during the Super Mystery Box Mania Events on Fridays. Like the Jackpot, they will see five rotating coin bags in the screen when they've earned a Super Jackpot.
Super Mega Jackpot
A Super Mega Jackpot has a 0.08% chance of being obtained and holds 1,500,000 . Like the regular Mega Jackpot, it replaces the highest amount of coins that can be obtained (300,000 ). They are only available during the Mega Jackpot Event, which occurs for 24 hours. The best method to obtain Super Mystery Boxes are to watch the ads in the Main Menu that give random rewards such as keys, mystery boxes, and more importantly super mystery boxes. If they're extremely lucky enough to hit this jackpot, then they would essentially have enough coins to fully upgrade all their powerups, buy almost if not all hoverboards, and buy some of the most expensive characters like Prince K and Brody .
Gallery

Super Jump
Super Jump is a hoverboard upgrade. When equipped, it gives the character the ability to jump higher than normal and pauses time for Super Sneakers . This ability is suggested for a few events such as Wordy Weekend and Tag Time Attack/Season Challenge .
List of Hoverboards

Super Mysterizer
Super Mysterizer is an exclusive power-up that only appears on Mystery Mondays. Like the Pogo Stick , it only appears on the tracks and cannot be upgraded. When the player collects one, it will give them one of four different abilities . They last for a limited time only. It first appeared in the edition and has appeared in every edition since.
Abilities
It can give the player one of four different abilities, they are:
A Random Hoverboard (Gives the player a random hoverboard from what's currently available in the inventory or the current season).
Double Coins (Temporarily makes all coins the player collects count as 2 instead of 1, similar to the unlimited double coins).
+10 Multiplier (Adds +10 to the current score multiplier).
Double Jump (Allows the player to double jump).
Trivia
There were originally going to be 5 abilities, the fifth one would transform the player into a random surfer. This ability would be called Surfer Swap, but it was never released in the game for unknown reasons.
In Subway Showdown this ability (random surfer) will be partially in the game, however you will only be able to transform into certain surfers from surprise objects (You will only be able to transform into Jake , Yutani , Spike , Miss Maia , Fantasma and Super Runner Fernando ).
In Subway Showdown this ability (random surfer) will be partially in the game, however you will only be able to transform into certain surfers from surprise objects (You will only be able to transform into Jake , Yutani , Spike , Miss Maia , Fantasma and Super Runner Fernando ).
In the update, the names of the mysteries appearing on the screen were removed, but this was reverted in the update.
If you're on hoverboard and get can get "Random Hoverboard" power-up, your hoverboard will change back after the power-up time is up. This power-up goes through your entire inventory of boards and the boards used for the current season as well. It selects 3-5 boards at random for every single run. This means that there is a chance that some of these boards will be randomly repeated for the same run.
You can get the same board you're using from this power-up .
You can get a board you don't have that is used for the current season from this power-up .
For unknown reasons, the random board will share the same ability that the player's hoverboard was using beforehand (except hoverboards with the Double Jump and Stay Low abilities).
You can get the same board you're using from this power-up .
You can get a board you don't have that is used for the current season from this power-up .
For unknown reasons, the random board will share the same ability that the player's hoverboard was using beforehand (except hoverboards with the Double Jump and Stay Low abilities).
You can combine "Double Coins" power-up with the regular Double Coins (offer) for a 4x bonus.
The "+10 multiplier" will add up your multiplier before 2X multiplier .
If you get "Double Jump" from this power-up after you activate a hoverboard with the same ability, or you activate it while this power-up is active, the power-up time limit will be paused until the hoverboard disappears. The same thing happens with Super Sneakers . Double Jump is also paused when using a Jetpack .

Navigation

Super Mystery Box
Super Mystery Box  is a type of prize box that can't be bought or collected (except during the Super Mystery Box Mania event). As the name suggests, the Super Mystery Box is a better or 'upgraded' version of the regular Mystery Box with bigger and better prizes. Some of the prizes can be multiplied by watching an ad.
Where to collect Super Mystery Boxes?
Completing Word Hunt for ≥5 days in a row.
Completing each mission set from set 30 (after reaching 30x multiplier).
Collecting normally during the run, only on Fridays .
Completing each Wordy Weekend challenge.
One of the possible rewards in Season Hunt .
Watching the third Daily Video .
One of the possible rewards for watching Daily Video normally.
Mystery Box Mania (1 Mini Mystery Box , 1 Mystery Box , 1 Super Mystery Box), awarded for every 10 ads watched in the game .
Collections Milestones.
Prizes
- Content chance if all Trophies and Character Tokens have been collected
Gallery

Super Mystery Box Mania
Super Mystery Box Mania is a 24-hour event (like most events) that causes Super Mystery Boxes to appear in the subway while running, making it easier to get better rewards.
They can normally only get Super Mystery Boxes by completing a Daily Challenge 5 times or more in a row , and completing a mission set when their Score Multiplier is x30. But with the event, they can get more Super Mystery Boxes. They appear in the same places as Season Hunt Tokens.
Starting in edition, this event is only effective every Friday (aka Fabulous Friday).
For unknown reasons, during , the event couldn't be seen in the "More" section on the Season Hunt (in this section, all events are shown), but in the edition, the bug was fixed.
Gallery
Events

Super Runner Challenge
Super Runner Challenge / Super Tag Team is a special event in the game. It is a Tag Team variant however unlike Tag Team in , , , and you only have one surfer you can play with and there also 5 stages. To unlock the Super Runners you must complete all 5 stages of the challenge. In a variant of the mode named Super Tag Team was made however this was mostly unlocked by a accidental bug and later made a full release in the amount of surfers in this mode varies on the edition (3 in 4 in - and 5 in ) as 2 of the Super Runners might not exist in the mode until the mode comes back again.
Releases
How To Play
Run with a brand new Super Runner character .
The character needs to cooldown every run.
Complete all stages and own your brand new Super Runner character .
Trivia
In , players were given another chance to get Super Runner Fresh due to a bug in . Super Tag Team was introduced in , then given in later updates such as and .

Super Runner Fernando
Super Runner Fernando is a limited character from the update. During the update, he can be obtained by completing the first 3 Stages in the Season Challenge. His Super Scout Outfit released in originally costing Stage 5 of Season Challenge, now it costs 150.
Appearance
Default Outfit
Super Runner Fernando is a Brazilian teenage boy from who has the look of a superhero with a super speed power, he has brown hair on which blue hair is painted on the left, and the costume itself is made in rich gray, red and purple colors. He has a zipper on his chest, a gray buckle on his waist, shorts in a rich gray color with a purple stripe at the bottom, black pants and tips with blue slits. The shoes are made in the colors of the suit. He has a black eye mask with an eye outline as blue.
Super Scout Outfit
Launched in , he now dresses in a scout uniform, on his body he has several emblems, a cap which has a blue and red lightning bolt drawing on it, a blue t-shirt, dark blue pants and brown shoes with yellow laces.
History
* An exact date was found where he appeared in, otherwise, the edition release date is assumed.

Super Runner Fresh
Super Runner Fresh is a limited character released in . In order to unlock him, the player needs to complete the 5th stage of the Super Runner City.
Appearance
Default Outfit
Fresh’s Super Runner outfit features olive glasses, black headphones with chartreuse X-marks on each pallet ornament, a mask, a hunter-green vest that is also a boombox with a speaker on the chest, three multicolored square buttons on the lower-right side of the front, and a cassette player on the lower-back with a loose belt dangling, a scarlet tank top, olive pants, and hunter-green boots. Fresh’s hair is dyed dark-green, and his nails are painted chartreuse. When on display, Fresh’s speaker produces visible sound waves.
History
* An exact date was found where he appeared in, otherwise, the edition release date is assumed.
Trivia

He is the 3rd character of 2022 to wear a face mask the 1st being Pink and the second being Super Runner Tricky
He is the second  surfer, the first being Bjarki , the third being Leif , and the fourth being Sindri .
He is the 3rd Super Runner character, the 1st being Super Runner Jake , the 2nd being Super Runner Tricky the 4th being Super Runner Yutani and the 5th being Super Runner Fernando .
He is the first special birthday surfer released in the 2020s for Fresh , the second being Hali .
His idle animation is similar to Charlie's idle animation.
Unlike Charlie , his boombox does not explode or mess up every time.
Unlike Charlie , his boombox does not explode or mess up every time.
He is the 3rd character to have a trail effect during the gameplay, the 1st being Super Runner Jake , and the 2nd being Super Runner Tricky .
During his first day of release, he was temporarily available for either 300 or , possibly due to a bug. This led to many people purchasing him before he was available.
The same day, the same thing happened to the other Super Runner characters, these being Super Runner Jake and Super Runner Tricky , both of which weren't supposed to be available for purchase at that time.
The same day, the same thing happened to the other Super Runner characters, these being Super Runner Jake and Super Runner Tricky , both of which weren't supposed to be available for purchase at that time.

He also appears to be younger.
Super Runner Fresh is the third Fresh variation. The first being Festive Fresh , the second being Pride Fresh , and the fourth being Elf Fresh .

Super Runner Jake
Super Runner Jake is a limited character exclusive to .
Appearance
Default Outfit
Jake's Super Runner outfit features a red hoodie, a baseball hat without the 'SubSurf' logo, a black domino mask, a safety belt, blue gloves and blue boots, and his hair and eyebrows are dyed blue. Super Runner Jake also holds two blue spray paint cans which are noticeably taped to his hands.

* An exact date was found where he appeared in, otherwise, the edition release date is assumed.

Super Runner Jake is the fourth Jake variation. The first being Zombie Jake , the second being Festive Jake , the third being Pride Jake , the fifth being Elf Jake , the sixth being Outatime Jake , and the seventh being Pixel Jake .

His weakness being food is a reference to the animated series.
He is the third voiced surfer , the first being Bonnie , the second being Cleo , the fourth being Fantasma , the fifth being Elf Jake , Elf Tricky , Elf Fresh , and Elf Yutani , the sixth being Soul , the seventh being Audrey , and the eighth being Billy Bean .

Super Runner Tricky
Super Runner Tricky is a limited character exclusive to the update. She could be obtained by completing all 5 stages in Super Runner City.
Appearance
Default Outfit
Tricky's Super Runner outfit features a golden earring, a black face mask, golden spikes on her shoulders connected by a golden chain; a white shirt, rich gray sleeved gloves; a golden belt with an aqua-blue gem, and black pants. In this new look, Tricky is sporting a new hairstyle, in which it looks somewhat similar to Lucy ’s. She has the majority of her hair flipped to the left and the other side of her head is shaved. She also wears rocket boots in the colors sky-blue, gold, and metallic gray. Her rocket boots emit aqua and yellow smoke.

Super Runner Yutani
Super Runner Yutani is a limited character from the update. She could be obtained by completing stage 5 of Super Runner City.
Appearance
Default Outfit
Yutani’s Super Runner outfit features a black domino mask, a purple coverall, a black utility belt with a golden buckle, black gloves, and green sneakers. A horseshoe magnet and a claw are attached to the rear of her belt. Yutani has a new hairstyle in this version, in which she dons space buns with two different colors on each side; purple on one half, and green on the other. Her eyebrows, too, are dyed purple and green.
History
* An exact date was found where she appeared in, otherwise, the edition release date is assumed.

She is the first surfer, the second being The Burger King , the third being Elf Jake , the fourth being Elf Fresh , the fifth being Elf Yutani , and the sixth being Alfie .
Super Runner Yutani is the fourth Super Runner, first being Super Runner Jake , second being Super Runner Tricky , third being Super Runner Fresh and fifth being Super Runner Fernando .
She has a slightly different facial texture than Yutani .
So far she is the only variation of Yutani without two extra hands as apart of her costume.
She is the first special birthday surfer released in the 2020s for Yutani , the second being Bruno .

Super Runners
Super Runners is a special anniversary event of Subway Surfers that celebrates the game's 10th anniversary.
Summary
Super Runners is an event that celebrates the 10th birthday anniversary of the game.
This event features 5 Super Runners: Super Runner Jake , the new version of Jake , the super leader of the group and Catrine 's Rival, Super Runner Tricky , the new version of Tricky , a super performance artist and Lawrence 's Rival, Super Runner Fresh , the new version of Fresh , a super DJ and Hali 's Rival, Super Runner Yutani , the new version of Yutani ,a super technologist and Bruno 's Rival, and Super Runner Fernando , the only Super Runner who does not have an original appearance. Except Super Runner Fernando , all of the Super Runners were surfers, where the Core Crew was from.
The 5 hoverboard of the Super Runners: Super Surfer , Trickster , Boombastic , Mag-Neat-O and Zuper Zapper .
They were released in , , , and respectively.
Trivia
This event featured 5 characters as the Super Runners Version of the 4 main characters of the game. Each Super Runner was released every update, but excluding Super Runner Fernando because he is a fan-made character.
Super Runners is the 1st event dedicated to the Subway Surfers Birthday, being dedicated to the 10th Anniversary, the second being the Rivals event the which was dedicated to the 11th Anniversary.
In the Rivals event, they came back for alongside with their Rivals.
Boombastic and Zuper Zapper were the only Super Runner Boards to not return in .
Super Runner Jake and Super Runner Fresh were the only Super Runners to not return in .

Super Runner Yutani is the super technologist of the Super Runners and the Rival of Bruno .
Super Runner Fernando is the super fan of the Super Runners and has no Rival.

Super Sneakers
Super Sneakers  (which look like this in-game) is a power-up in Subway Surfers . When collected, the player will wear the shoes, and gain a jump boost temporarily. The player can now jump on top of trains , and jump over obstacles they would usually have to roll under to dodge (i.e. Tallest Barriers ). The bottom of the shoes can vacuum coins under them just like a magnet , allowing the player to collect coins under them (in the lane they are on only) without even touching them, they will also be able to collect other power-ups when under those shoes. If the player collects a Jetpack before the sneakers run out, the timer for the sneakers are paused, and it is not reactivated until the Jetpack runs out, just like the timer for the hoverboards . If they are upgraded, they can last until 30 seconds.
Trivia
When the players ride a Jetpack with a pair of Super Sneakers, every coin they'll be collecting mid-air gives them Double coins. This occurs in older versions of Subway Surfers . See  Glitches  for more info (glitches may not work).
If the player gets the Super Sneakers while the Guard is chasing them, the Guard will slow down. However, this does not apply if the Super Sneakers are still active beforehand.
If the player jumps with the Super Sneakers while they are under a bridge, the roof of the bridge will prevent them from jumping any higher until they are out of the bridge.
If the player jumps with the Super Sneakers before reaching the bridge, they'll slam their heads into the bridge and get caught by The Guard . But if they are on a train, they can reach the bridge.
Occasionally, a player might get stuck there, and the only thing they can do to get out of there is to swipe down. This action will be counted as stumbling, meaning that the inspector will be right behind them after that.
If the player jumps with the Super Sneakers before reaching the bridge, they'll slam their heads into the bridge and get caught by The Guard . But if they are on a train, they can reach the bridge.
Occasionally, a player might get stuck there, and the only thing they can do to get out of there is to swipe down. This action will be counted as stumbling, meaning that the inspector will be right behind them after that.
Occasionally, a player might get stuck there, and the only thing they can do to get out of there is to swipe down. This action will be counted as stumbling, meaning that the inspector will be right behind them after that.
With the Super Sneakers, they could go to places they couldn't normally go, such as on top of tunnels, which helps collect Weekly Hunt or Season Hunt tokens.
The shoes have an "Upwards arrow symbol" which resembles the ability of the sneakers.
Before the update, players were able to collect Weekly Hunt tokens from under the shoes without touching them.
However, after the update, that ability was removed, making it harder to collect them.
You can still collect Coins , Keys , and power-ups from under the shoes without touching them.
However, after the update, that ability was removed, making it harder to collect them.
You can still collect Coins , Keys , and power-ups from under the shoes without touching them.
If the player jumps right after you collect the Super Sneakers, they will discover that their Character will jump very low.
Similarly, if they jump right when the sneakers wear out, they will jump very high without wearing the Super Sneakers, but they will fall very quickly too.
Similarly, if they jump right when the sneakers wear out, they will jump very high without wearing the Super Sneakers, but they will fall very quickly too.
The Super Sneakers icon is slightly different from the ones to collect on the tracks.
This, the Pogo Stick , and Jetpack are the only power-ups in the game that lets the player's character reach the sky.
While that, the Bouncer board is the only hoverboard to do so.
While that, the Bouncer board is the only hoverboard to do so.
When players collect a Super Sneakers power and then activates a hoverboard with the Super Jump ability, the Super Sneakers’ power-up will not deplete. This is useful when they are trying to earn the High Jumper Achievements.
The Super Sneakers don't return in Subway Surfers City as a power-up, instead replaced by Bubble Gum . However, it makes an appearance in the background as a hologram in Sunrise Boulevard with a new design that looks more like a real sneaker.
In , they received a new design.
The timer for the Super Sneakers doesn't go down until the board's timer is over is for boards that have Super Jump . (Ex. Monster , Freestyler )
When the Super Sneakers is over and you swipe up when it perfectly ends, you will jump really high.
In v3.57.1, the Super Sneakers' sound is bugged and higher-pitched. When you swipe up, you can't hear the sound, but when you swipe down, you can hear it.

Super Speed
Super Speed (Originally "Surf Faster") and Speed Up are hoverboard powers, which can be default or as a hoverboard upgrade . This power makes the character move faster.
Super Speed makes the character run 1.25x faster and increases the field of view, while Speed Up makes the character run 1.125x faster, and does not increase the field of view.

Super Surfer
Super Surfer is a limited hoverboard from . Like any other hoverboard, it protects your character from any obstacles for 30 seconds. When crashed, it will explode and need a few seconds to recharge before it can be used again. You have to complete Stage 3 from Super Runner City to unlock it. It is the first of 5 Super Runners boards and is the board of Super Runner Jake .

It is an Orange, White, Light Blue, and Blue hoverboard. It has a patch on the side as a decoration.

Surf as Yourself
"Surf as Yourself" is a song in Subway Surfers sung by Riley . It was released in , on June 3rd, 2024. It's available in the game's boombox as background music and also in music streaming services, like Spotify or Apple Music. The soundtrack ( Surf as Yourself (San Francisco 2024) ) is based on this song, but it doesn't have lyrics, only vocalizing.
https://subwaysurf.fandom.com/wiki/File:Subway-Surfers-San-Francisco-2024-Surf-as-Yourself-Soundtrack.mp3

Credits
Trivia
It's the third song (lyric soundtrack) in the game, after Bling (in ) and Higher (in ).
This song's genre is europop.
This song is dedicated to the Pride Month.
Unlike previous songs, it's the 1st song which consists of only one repeated phrase, the 2nd begin Vamos Dançar (in ) and 3rd begin Colors (in ).
This is the only Subway Surfers ' song that has no official lyric video, unlike previous and subsequent songs.

Surfer Pass
The Surfer Pass is a feature which was released in beta updates of 4.0.1/4.0.2 and 4.1.0. The player had to complete missions to receive their rewards including hoverboards , characters , and portraits . It replaced the Season Hunt used previously.
Characters
Surfer Pass
Challenges
Trivia
In the Chinese version, this feature is called Red Book Pass .
The Red Book Pass Plus (Surfer Pass Plus) in the Chinese version cost 68 instead of .
The Red Book Pass Plus (Surfer Pass Plus) in the Chinese version cost 68 instead of .
This feature received backlash due to some free stuff such as Portraits , characters , outfits , boards , and challenges like , becoming unobtainable without paying along with a removal of Event Coins .
Since , it has been coming back for some users.
Paid characters (either Surfer Pass Plus or Surfer Pass) will return after 4-5 updates as free and can be get by completing challenges. (Like original: Mega Characters come back after 4-5 updates with event coins .)
It also has its own bug: It said "Reach Tier 60 on the Season Hunt to unlock." (It says Season Hunt instead of Surfer Pass Tier or something)

Surveillance
Surveillance is the seventh episode of Subway Surfers: The Animated Series .
Synopsis
When Yutani reveals her latest invention, Tricky insists they must keep it a secret. But Jake has greater plans, plans that could put them all in danger.

Sweet Chili
Sweet Chili is a limited hoverboard released in . Like any other hoverboard, it protects your character from any obstacles for 30 seconds. When crashed, it will explode and need a few seconds to recharge before it can be used again.
Appearance
It is basically a skull with three exhaust pipes on the back and one on the side with flame pattern on it and it also has a red flaming effect coming from inside of the pipes.
Trivia
Sweet Chili is the fourth board after Kitty , Cherry and Sakura .
It is the 15th board of 2023 , after Kite , Jamanta , Future Wave , Big Boss Board , Color Shift , Flying Horse , 11th Birthday , Danger Dasher , Tagger , I-Scream , Viperfish , Fan-tastic , Ghastly Glider , and Carp Diem .
It returned for the first time in in the Spooky Sprint challenge.
In , it costs in a bundle offer with Yuto , Akira , and Override .
In and , it costs in Offer Trails .
In the Chinese Version, this board is 2023 board and called Thai Cool Spicy (泰酷辣) .
It's Yuto 's first board and Cathy 's second board . Cathy ’s first and third boards are Beelzebook and Koinobori .
Its trail is bugged with a black square if you selected it in player profile .

Trivia

It is Marie Macaron 's board .

Sweetheart
Sweetheart is a limited hoverboard in Subway Surfers . It was released with . Like any other hoverboard, it will protect you from crashing for 30 seconds. When crashed, the board will explode and recharge for a few seconds before it can be used again. It costs 2.99.
Appearance
The hoverboard has the appearance of a pink cloud with a heart trail.
History
Sweetheart was originally released with costing 2.99.
In , it cost 50,000.
It came back on costing 50.
In , Sweetheart returns for 2.99.
In , Sweetheart returns for 50.
In , Sweetheart returns for or 350.000 with Harumi .
In , Sweetheart returns for or 200 with Coco and her Art Outfit.
After 3 days returning in for 50, Sweetheart is available for 50,000.
In , Sweetheart returns for .
In and , Sweetheart returns in the for those who already had Bunny .
For unknown reasons, it did not return in and .
Trivia
It is first hoverboard special for the Valentine's Day.
It is the second board, the first being Rose , the third being Paint Palette , the fourth being Parasol , the fifth being Sporty Summer , the sixth being Heavy Metal , the seventh being Champ , and the eighth being Arrow .
It is also the first hoverboard to be purchased with real money by $2.99.
Sweetheart is the second board resembles a cloud, the first is Thunderbolt , with the third being Color Cloud and fourth being Snow Cloud .
It is Coco ’s 2nd board. Her first board is Rose .
In the Chinese Version, it's a 2018 board, and it's called Romantic Sweetheart (浪漫甜心) and it's labelled as Valentine Day Board .

Sylvester Rishøj Jensen
Sylvester Rishøj Jensen is a Danish entrepreneur and animator who is the co-founder of SYBO Games .
Biography
Sylvester Rishøj Jensen was born in Denmark and studied at The Animation Workshop, getting a Bachelor in Character Animation and a Bachelor in 3D Art Professional Training Course, both in 2009.
In the same year, he and Bodie Jahn-Mulliner directed Trainbombing .
In 2010, they created SYBO Games . Rishøj Jensen became the CCO (Chief Commercial Officer) and Founding Partner of that company, positions that would maintain him until 2018, year where he left these two positions. However, he was still Board member of the videogames developer, and in August 2022 he totally quit SYBO Games .
In 2020, he founded Arkaia ApS project, a foundation that was finally founded in 2024. It "aims to support activities that enhance human existential security through contributions to greater and long-term resilience in the Earth's biosphere as well as human habitat and livelihoods".
From 2021 to 2022, Rishøj Jensen studied again, but this time at University of Copenhagen , studying Nanoscience and Master's Degree, and Climate Management in Plant Production and Research.
References

Tag Time Attack
Tag Time Attack is a game mode (a type of the Marathon Challenge ) released in bug-fix version (and then it was named Timed Tag Team ). In , it was officially released and had 5 stages, after completing each of which you could get a character and/or a board (some challenges in the following updates are regular, that is, they don't have special rewards and consist of only 1 or 3 stages, or special, which have special rewards and consist of 5 stages). This challenge can only be played with certain characters .
Technically, it's Tag Team , but now there's a timer in the game: the player has 1 minute to run and can replenish the time by collecting , which add +10 seconds. If the time runs out — the game is over.
The minimum required score to complete the event is usually 2.5M (if you are at 30 or higher).
How to Play?
Pick up hourglasses to increase your time.
All points are added up for your score.
Your surfers need to rest after each run.
Switch surfers to keep in going and get bigger rewards!

Tagbot
Tagbot is a character that can be unlocked by purchasing it with 12,000. it is the second cheapest buyable character in Subway Surfers, the first cheapest being Lucy . Its first outfit, the Space Outfit, can be bought for 30. Its second outfit, the Toy Outfit, can be bought for 30.
Appearance
Default Outfit
Tagbot is a grey robot with three floating segments (2 semispheres, 1 sphere for the head) that wears a cap that is the same one as Jake's . The cap has the words "Sub Surf" on it. During the update, it received a redesign, giving it eyebrows. These were removed later.
In the update, it received 2 new outfits, which are the Space Outfit and the Toy Outfit, each cost 30.
Space Outfit
In Tagbot's Space Outfit, it has been given blue stripes and a satellite and antenna. They also have a few minor redesigns. From onwards, the green parts became blue. This outfit costs 30.
Toy Outfit
In Tagbot's Toy Outfit, it is painted red and with buttons on its chest, with the added detail of a twisting key, that players twist in order to make the toy move around. Its Toy Outfit costs 30.
Trivia
Tagbot was the first buyable character who received outfits .
It's the only robot in the game that has two outfits.
Tagbot is the first robot character in the game. The others robot characters are Boombot , Rabbot , Monkbot , Tankbot , Spacebot , Frankette , Gingerbot , Dummy , Spraybot , and Teabot .
Tagbot's Space Outfit slightly resembles the droid R2-D2 from Star Wars because of the blue parts and robot pieces on the head. The name of the outfit is also probably a reference to Star Wars as that franchise is set in space.
The head segment of this character is removable and can be used as a basketball, which it does in one of its animations.
It gets a +5 Multiplier Bonus for Special Thursdays such as: , , , , , , , , , , , , and .
In , it came back in 2 bundles, with Monkbot and Amy , and its Space Outfit, Aero and Discovery .
It got a +3 Multiplier Bonus for Multiplier Bonus in .
In , it costs 12,000 individually, or in a Black Friday bundle with its Space Outfit, Aero , and Discovery .
In , it costs 12,000 individually, or in the Board Awakens Bundle bundle with its Space Outfit and Teleporter .
It and Alba both have an outfit called Space Outfit.
It is the second character to have "Sub Surf" written on its outfit. Other characters are Jake , Nick , Alicia (in Tracksuit Outfit), Callum , Zayn , and Leif .
It is the first unknown gender character in Subway Surfers history, not counting the Dog .
It used to have eyebrows back in , but these were removed in a later update.
In the Chinese Version, it's a limited character.
It, Rabbot , Tankbot , Gingerbot , and Dummy are the only robots to have outfits.

Taha
Taha is a limited character released in the update. He costs 25,000.
His 1st outfit, Tropi-Taha outfit, can be obtained at level 20 in the Season Hunt in , (collect 210 /Now 150).
His 2nd outfit, Chili-Taha outfit, can be obtained at the stage 5 in the Season Challenge in (Now 150).
Appearance
Default Outfit
Taha is a Moroccan teenage boy and painter from with black hair and brown eyes. He wears a yellow tank top under a baby blue jacket, light blue jeans, a black fedora, and blue shoes with yellow laces. He also holds a pencil in his ear but does not carry it while playing. In his animation, he draws Aina in her Blue Outfit. He also has a gray watch if you look close enough. His clothes have many details, such as stitching and seams.
Default Outfit after update
Taha is a Moroccan teenage boy and painter from with brown hair and brown eyes. He wears an orange-yellow tank top under a baby blue jacket (notice that there is less detail prior to his original appearance) blue jeans (less detail like the jacket), a black fedora, and blue shoes with orange laces. He also holds a pencil in his ear but does not carry it while playing. In his animation, he draws Aina in her Blue Outfit. Now his eyebrows are brown and whole (that is, they do not have incisions), the skin has become darker, the fedora has become bigger, there is no watch on the wrist, there are fewer details on the clothes.
Tropi-Taha Outfit
Launched in , he wears a large hat with a pineapple design, three green leaves on the tip as well as on the sides, his eyebrows are now gray, he has round yellow sunglasses, he has a yellow vest just like his shorts. He also wears a green t-shirt, has a green bracelet on his right arm and two orange and yellow bracelets on his left arm, and also has yellow flip-flops. In his animation, he draws a Pineapple.
Chilli Taha Outfit
Launched in , he wears a large hat with a design, he has green sunglasses, he has a brown vest with chili peppers drawings, and has green shorts. He also wears a red t-shirt, has a black bracelet on his right arm, and wears a white necklace and also has some gray shoes. In his animation, he draws a roasted tomato salsa recipe.
History
* An exact date was found where he appeared in, otherwise, the edition release date is assumed.
Trivia
He's the fifth African surfer after Jasmine , Zuri , Aina and Salma .
Prior to Hamza , he was the first and only African male surfer released in the game.
He held the portrait of Aina ’s Blue Outfit on his left hand. Which means it is possible that he is Aina 's boyfriend as shown in Bazaar Breezers bundle in .
He's the second surfer, the first being Salma , the third being Hamza , the fourth being Faiza , and the fifth being Teabot .
Taha is the seventh surfer who have their names starting with alphabet 'T' after Tagbot , Tasha , Tony , Tom , Tankbot , and Trym .
Taha is the 25th character released in 2021 , after Frankette , Spacebot , Ox , Rat , Qin , Hou , Zayn , Darryl , Lana , Bonnie , Buzz , Trym , Pridebot , Pride Jake , Pride Fresh , Pride Spike , Pride Tricky , Pride Yutani , Pride Frank , Parkour Rin , Alexandre , Akira , Koral , and Cleo .

He is also the ninth surfer to receive the full set of outfit in 2024 after Moira , George , Hasina , Cathy , Koral , Cleo , Phoenix , and Pink .

Tainá
Tainá is a limited character released in the update. She is the 164th character in the game.
Appearance
Default Outfit
Tainá is a 16-year-old Afro-Brazilian teenage girl, samba dancer, and judoka from . A dark skinned, she wears a light gray crop top with a colourful graphic, she also wears light gray sweatpants and a green belt. In the character selection menu she's barefoot, but she wears light gray sneakers with orange and green details in-game.
Judoka Outfit
Launched in , she now wears a purple and blue band on her head, 2 gold earrings, a green martial arts suit, a yellow belt and a purple badge with a light red heart on it. When you select her in this outfit she will wear purple shoes.
History
* An exact date was found where she appeared in, otherwise, the edition release date is assumed.
Trivia
Tainá is the 10th surfer released in 2023 after Rabbit , Soul , Tutti , Frutti , Spraybot , Catrine , Rain , Alfie , and Bruno .
Tainá is the third surfer after Carmen , and Super Runner Fernando .
She's the fourth Brazilian surfer after Edison , Carmen , and Super Runner Fernando .

Tainá is an indigenous name originating from the Tupi-Guarani languages. It means "star".
In the character menu, she is barefoot, but in-game, she wears sneakers.
Prior to Guard King , she was the last surfer to be available in the Season Hunt .
She is introduced as “Taina” in the trailer video, but her actual name is Tainá. This is most likely because the font SYBO TV uses in the current trailers don’t support Spanish/Portuguese letters nor special characters.

Tang Yuan
Tang Yuan is a character released in . She is the 217th character in the game and 2nd surfer of 2025 .
Appearance
Default Outfit
Tang Yuan (汤圆) is a 14-year-old Chinese girl from Luoyang ( ) / Chang'an. She has crimson red eyes, purple eyeliner for makeup, and her black hair has red and yellow tips. On her head, there's a brown bowl of tangyuans (sweet round rice dumplings). She wears a purple, white, and orange-colored off-shoulder top with tiny orange bows and white long sleeves. There's orange-colored armbands with red and yellow stripes of snake scale print on both of her arms. On her right hand is a large metal spoon that has a tangyuan with tiny face on it (which was named Larry by the fans), but she doesn't hold it during the gameplay. She has black above-the-knee shorts, over which she has two pieces of hanging fabric grasped with a dark grey belt, to which is tied a grey and yellow hexagonal handbag. Lastly, she has a pair of orange socks and dark gray sneakers, which has white soles and in brown and yellow accents.

Tankbot
Tankbot is a limited edition character from . It was chosen from 3 character designs in Instagram by p3n_0f_ishaan . It can be unlocked with  20.
Appearance
Default Outfit
Tankbot is a robot made in with a body almost similar to that of a fish tank/aquarium, but in green and blue and yellow. It has a head with an aquarium, containing an orange fish inside.
Inca Outfit
Launched in . Its body is maroon, blue and yellow. Its body now has patterns on it and the fish in the aquarium is now green with orange fins.
History
* An exact date was found where it appeared in, otherwise, the edition release date is assumed.
Trivia
Tankbot is one of the only robots in the game. The others being Tagbot , Boombot , Rabbot , Monkbot , Spacebot , Frankette , Pridebot , Gingerbot , Dummy , Spraybot , and Teabot .
It is the first South American robot surfer in Subway Surfers history.
Tankbot is the second character unlocked in the Season Hunt in its released, with the first being Festive Fresh in update.
Tankbot is the first character to be made by a fan from an art contest, the second being Koral , the third being Bob the Blob , the fourth being Super Runner Fernando , the fifth being Spraybot , the sixth being Hammy-Bee , the seventh being Dr. Flyman , the eighth being Snowy , the ninth being Valentina , and the tenth being Berta .
Tankbot is the fifth South American surfer in the game, the first being Carmen , the second being Edison , the third being Carlos , the fourth being Sofia , the sixth being Super Runner Fernando , the seventh being Bruno , the eighth being Tainá , the ninth being Yutani Harlequin Toad , the teenth being J Balvin , the eleventh being Hammy-Bee , the twelfth being Rebeca Roar , and the thirteenth being Gabriel .
Tankbot and Carlos both have an outfit called Inca Outfit.
Tankbot is the first character without a face, there is only a fish in its helmet. The second character without a face is Dummy , the third one is Soul , and the fourth one is Teabot .

Tara
Tara is a character released during . She is the 240th character in the game and the 25th surfer of 2025 .
Appearance
Default Outfit
Tara is a 16-year-old British teenage girl from that studies in the Uptown College of Creatives, who are well-known for their red and blue-colored uniforms. With that, she wears a blue-accented red suit with a large red and blue-striped neck tie, short gray skirt, as well as a pair of red and blue-striped long socks and shoes. For accessories, she uses a blue hat for her high side ponytail, matching it her with gold, asymmetrical ear piercings.

Tasha
Tasha is a character that can be unlocked by purchasing with 30,000.
Natasha Blackwell is a real name, but, the crew calls her "Tasha".
Appearance
Default Outfit
Tasha is a young woman who somewhat resembles Tricky, wearing a white cropped tank top that bares her abs and belly button, white socks with red stripes, red sneakers, and loose-fitting blue dolphin shorts over her black knee-length leggings. She also has her blonde hair tied with a blue hair band in a ponytail, along with a red headband. During the update, she received a new outfit called the Cheer Outfit that costs 15. Her second outfit, the Gym Outfit was introduced in . The outfit costs 25.
Cheer Outfit
In her Cheer Outfit, her hair now changed to pigtail style, with her hair going to both left and right of her head. She now wears a purple cropped tank top and recolored dolphin shorts, though her leggings, socks, and sneakers remained the same. She also seems to be holding a purple pompom.
Gym Outfit
In her Gym Outfit, her hair style changes again; one great stash of her hair is sticking out of the right of her hair; and she has side swept bangs. She now wears seafoam green hoop earrings and her top was recolored to coral. Her shorts are now outlined neon green and her socks are also changed to knee-high coral socks. However, her sneakers and leggings still remain the same.
Limited Outfits (Variants)
Crewmate Tasha ( )
Trivia
In , Tasha gets a new animation style.
She gets +5 Multiplier Bonus for Special Thursdays such as , , , , , and .
Tasha gets +3 Multiplier Bonus for Multiplier Bonus in .
Lucy is her sister, according to the official post for Siblings Day.
She costs 100 Surfer credits, which is equivalent to 500 Keys in Subway Surfers City .
In the Chinese version, her Outfits received a little change since , to cover her middlriff and chest. This change applies to a lot of female characters.
Tasha, Jake , Tricky , Fresh , Spike , Yutani , Boombot , Rin , Frank , Miss Maia , King and Dino are the only characters in the game who have limited edition counterparts, Zombie Jake ( Halloween update), Elf Tricky ( Christmas update), Festive Fresh ( update), Festive Jake , Festive Tricky , Festive Spike , Festive Frank and Festive Yutani ( update), Pride Jake , Pride Tricky , Pride Fresh , Pride Spike , Pride Yutani , Pride Frank and Pridebot ( update), Spacebot ( update), Gingerbot ( update), Parkour Rin ( update), Super Runner Jake ( update), Pride Miss Maia and Super Runner Tricky ( ), Super Runner Fresh ( update), Super Runner Yutani ( update), Elf Jake , Elf Yutani and Elf Fresh ( update), Yutani Harlequin Toad ( update), Outatime Jake ( update), Pixel Jake , Ballerina Tricky and Guard King ( update), Dinorange ( update), Chicken Jake and Mallard Tricky ( update), 8 Ball Jake ( update), Crewmate Jake , Crewmate Tricky , Crewmate Spike , Crewmate King and Crewmate Tasha ( update), Frank Noir ( update).

Tasha (Subway Surfers City)
Tasha is a revamped version of the Subway Surfers character Tasha . Despite previously being a Subway Pass exclusive character, currently in Subway Surfers City , she can be unlocked as a jackpot in the Super Boxes.
Appearance
Tasha is a teenager with blonde hair tied with a pink hair band in a ponytail and teal eyes. She wears a denim colored oversized varsity jacket, a pink cheerleader dress underneath, dark pink leggings, pink leg warmers and white sneakers with pink details. Lastly, her nails are painted pink.
Power-ups
Bubbly Personality
Tasha gets a bubblegum when activating a board.
Sugar Rush
Tasha gets extra multiplier when a bubblegum is active.
Double Gum
Tasha has 50% chance to get another bubblegum when one pops.
Trivia
Tasha is the 13th character in Subway Surfers City .
She is the 10th character who originally came from Subway Surfers . the first being Jake , second being Tricky , third being Fresh , fourth being Yutani , fifth being Miss Maia , sixth being Ninja One , seventh being Tagbot , eight being Spike and the ninth being Ella .
Her current design has made an appearance as a profile picture in Subway Surfers Blast .
In the Subway Surfers Blast files she’s registered as Trisha , which indicates she was originally going to be renamed to Trisha , however she ended up being kept as Tasha .
Her animation featuring a pink bubblegum resembles Mina .
She appears to be younger than her Subway Surfers counterpart .
She was released in the Super Box, along with Sweet Street , in the very beginning of Season 6.
In Season 6, Tasha got a new upgrade animation.
Her previous upgrade animation was reused from Jake , most likely being a placeholder.
Her previous upgrade animation was reused from Jake , most likely being a placeholder.
Tasha has 7 exclusive animations in game.
Some of them can be found in the character menu: her intro, breathe and upgrade idles.
The others can be seen in gameplay, one being the run animation and the rest are three different jumps exclusive to her character.

Her hoverboard is Sweet Street .

Teabot
Teabot is a limited character released in . It is the 187th character of the game.
Appearance
Teabot is a 1-year-old Moroccan tea robot from . It has a tea kettle as its head. It also has a body similar to Tankbot 's body, especially the hands.
History
* An exact date was found where it appeared in, otherwise, the edition release date is assumed.
Trivia
It, Faiza and Hamza are the 3rd, 4th and 5th surfers after Salma and Taha .
It is the first robot surfer released in 2024 .
It is the first African robot surfer in Subway Surfers history.
It is the second surfer featured in , the first being Red Panda .
It is the 12th surfer released in 2024 after Thunder , Skye , Azura , Dragon , Stella , Astra , Lucky , Meggan , Red Panda , Hamza , and Faiza . It is also the 3rd African surfers released in 2024 after Hamza and Faiza .
Its body is similar to Tankbot 's.
It is the youngest surfer of 2024 .
It is the 15th surfer that started with 'T' after Tricky , Tasha , Tagbot , Tony , Taha , Tom , Trym , Tankbot , Tiger , The Burger King , Tutti , Tainá , Thursday , and Thunder .
It, Hamza and Faiza were not available in Subway Studio until 3.33.1, like Alba , Carlos , Cathy , Lucky , Meggan , and Red Panda .
In the "MEET THE CREW" page, Teabot is the first robot surfer to have a known age, being 1 year old and is the youngest character in the game. Teabot also uses all pronouns (He/She/They/It), meaning that Teabot is gender-fluid, making it the sixth robot surfer to have known gender after Monkbot , Rabbot , Spraybot , Captain Flash , and Clockwork Johnny .
This would make it the first genderfluid, then Hammy-Bee being the second and Riley being the third.

Its birthday is between March 21 and April 19 (the exact date is unknown).

Unlike King , Clementine , and Holly , it is a robot.
It is the first LGBTQIA2S+ child to be in the game.
It is the first genderfluid child in the game.
Unlike King , Clementine , and Holly , it is a robot.

Team Takeover
Team Takeover is an event introduced in , which is a rivalry between two characters (the leaders of the teams) and other character members of the teams.
The characters are divided into two teams: Red Team and Green Team . Every team have its own representing game mode. The Red Team takes the Challenge, while the Green Team takes the Challenge.
The Coins are recolored for this challenge. In , the coins becomes red; while in , the coins becomes green.
The Mystery Box also gets a new look, where it has a VS logo instead of question mark. In , the box becomes red; while In , the box becomes green.
The final reward includes either 10 red boxes or 10 green boxes, with other rewards.
Prizes
The final Mystery Box prize of this challenge contains the characters of the winner team.
During
During
Trivia
This is the first challenge introduced in the game where there's a competition or rivalry between the Characters .
Dinorange appears in this challenge as a rare prize since . Before that, it is an unused character found in game files.
If you already have every character on the winning team, you will get 10 from the final Mystery Box instead.
This is currently the only challenge that is not present in the Chinese Version.
Gallery

Teddy
Teddy is the twelfth limited hoverboard in Subway Surfers . Like the rest of the boards, it protects you from crashing for 30 seconds. When crashed, the board will explode and recharge for 5 seconds before it can be reused. Its release was in . It can be bought for 50,000, like all other limited hoverboards.
Appearance
The hoverboard has the appearance of a brown teddy bear whose eyes seem to be closed. It also has black claws and seems to be smiling.
Trivia
It is the first board, the 2nd being Leaf .
In , it cost 50,000.
In , it cost 30.
In , it cost 50,000.
In , it cost 20,000.
In , it cost 20,000.
In , it cost 20.
In , it cost 20.
Prior to , this was one of the rarest boards in the game since it didn't return after 2021 .
Though the Leaf , Sleigh , and Mimi returned in 2022 and 2023 , Teddy never returned in 2022 or 2023 , neither did Alex and Nicolai for unknown reasons.
It may have been because of the war that is still going on between Russia and Ukraine.

Teleporter
Teleporter is a hoverboard which is introduced in edition. Its special ability is zapping sideways; that is, transporting from one lane to another instantaneously.
Appearance
This is mostly a cosmetic change. It has the appearance of a circular blue-black colored disc.
Trivia
It is one of the boards (including special abilities only) which does not have upgrades.
It is the first board to be shaped like a circle.
In certain editions, it was available for free trial during the Wild Wednesday event.
It's the last non limited board of the 2010's decade.
The design of it is heavily based on the Identity (or Light) Discs from Tron: Legacy .
The Teleporter board was added to the game on 10 July 2014.

Temple Rush
Temple Rush is a hoverboard released in . Like any other hoverboard, it protects the character from crashing for 30 seconds. When crashed, it will need to recharge for a few seconds before it can be used again.
Appearance
A golden hoverboard that features an Indonesian dragon/lion head mask at the front, and a red-colored rear. There are two large yellow boosters attached to the sides, both of which having black, pointy ends.

Terrific Tuesday
Terrific Tuesday is a limited event on Subway Surfers that is always available on Tuesdays. The utility of this event is win the jackpot in the boxes with more coins that the normal. With the Normal Box, you can win 900,000 and with the Super Mystery Box, you can win 1,500,000 . The 14th February of 2017 (during the edition) is an exception, it was the Valentine's Day Special Terrific Tuesday, making the jackpot 1,430,000 with the Normal Box and 2,240,000 with the Super Mystery Box.
Trivia
In , was revealed the percentage to win the 900,000 (the Mega Jackpot) is 0.11% (that means the probability of win is 1 in 909).
However, it was changed later to 0.01% (that means the probability of win is 1 in 10,000).

The Burger King
The Burger King is a limited character released on . He can be unlocked by completing all 3 stages of the Burger King Challenge .
Appearance
Default Outfit
The Burger King is dressed like a king, with a big gold crown that has the Burger King logo and 3 red jewels on top. He also has a red short-sleeved robe over a brown long-sleeve, short, brown parachute pants, and white tights. He also wears a gold Burger King medallion and black buckle shoes. His look is complete with poofy brown hair all over his head and face.

The Green Flame
The Green Flame is a hoverboard released in . Like any other hoverboard, it protects the character from crashing for 30 seconds. When crashed, it will need to recharge for a few seconds before it can be used again.
Appearance
A mainly green-colored hoverboard that has a lighter shade of flame design, which stretches from the front to the middle of its base. It also has a spiky front and a blue rear. Beside its two green "wings" on the sides are two boosters that emits real, glowing green flames when used in the game.

Toaxx
Toaxx is a character released in . She is the 276th character in the game and the 14th surfer of 2026 .
Appearance
Default Outfit
Toaxx is a 16-year-old Danish female toad from The Sewers ( ) that has a purple luminescent skin, with random spots all over her body having darker hues. Her head and facial features include a bubbly and spiky hair, thick eyebrows, sharp eyelashes, a singular sharp teeth, and eyes having a neon green iris surrounded by a red border. Under her denim vest is a black shirt with torn sleeves and a radioactive symbol. Meanwhile, her purple shorts have neon green accents, and her sneakers are in muted purple and gray, which has laces and are held by brown soles. For accessories, she has spiked bracelets on both of her wrists.
Trivia

Token Box
Token Box is a type of Mystery Box that gives the player Character Tokens , making it easier to unlock the 4 default Token Characters and the Jake's Star Outfit . A Token Box can be purchased with 6, but once players finish the tutorial, they are given a one-time free Token Box.
When you collected all the tokens and unlocked all the characters and the outfits, it will allow you to purchase one more box, but it gives your 6 back and you won’t be able to buy a token box again.
Items
6 (when unlocking all tokens to unlock all outfits)

Tom
Tom (Tom Brady)  is a limited edition character from . He can be unlocked with  50.
Appearance
Default Outfit
Tom is a 48 years old American man who is a football player from San Mateo, California. In the game, he wears a maroon jersey with the number "12" on it. He wears gray pants, white wristbands and maroon and white sneakers.
History
Trivia
He is a NFLPA player from real life .
He, Patrick , Saquon , Odell , Lamar and Dak are the NFLPA players/group.
He is also the oldest character from the NFLPA players/group.
However as of February 2023, Tom has retired from the NFLPA for good.

Tony
Tony was launched as a limited character from . He was also the first limited character in the World Tour series. He could originally be purchased during with 95,000 but now he costs 100,000. Both of his outfits can be purchased for 75, respectively.
Appearance
Default Outfit
Tony is an American teenage boy from who has sunglasses, black fingerless gloves, yellow work boots, zebra-striped socks, golden necklace, spiky haircut, light blue jeans and a navy blue sleeveless jacket with a white t-shirt underneath.
Folk Outfit
Launched in , his jacket changes to black, his t-shirt turns dark red, his boots become black. His socks are colored yellow, and his sunglasses and gold chain have been replaced by a ochre panama hat with a black band.
Game Outfit
Launched in , he wears what a baseball fan would typically wear, which includes a bright blue cap, a vivid yellow shirt, charcoal-colored sweatpants, and a dark royal blue and white jumper.

Toolbars
Toolbar are the lines of various buttons in Subway Surfers which help navigate the game. The buttons have specific purposes which include opening menus and information about content.
Toolbar are part of the controls in Subway Surfers .
Each toolbar has a specific purpose.
Main Toolbar
Me Toolbar
The Toolbar consists of buttons to view the Characters , Boards and Power-Ups' upgrades menus.
Trivia
The Toolbar has been changed on the update, later on and later on an unknown date.

Top Run
Top Run is a challenge that people get every week. This event gives out medals or may not.
Ranks and Prizes
History
The Top Run was added to the game on June 19th, 2014 with the update.
On February 12th, 2015 ( ), the Diamond Medals were added to Top Run.
Since 2.20.1 , the background is no longer the rainbow, instead it is based on the player placement.
On May 16th, 2022 ( ), the characters no longer put their hand on their right hip.
On February 2023 ( ), there is a new Top Run pose and animations, and the previous 3 Top Run animations are removed.
In 3.28.2 , the original Top Run profiles were removed and replaced with new profiles that only show the core crew.
Since , a new Top Run UI has been slowly rolling out to some users. This made the Top Run page have a shinier appearance, made King holding the Gold and Diamond medals as the banner, the medals now have a dark blue outline, and on the medal claim page the background color will be based on the color of the medal the player got.

Train Riders
Train Riders was an endless action video game developed and published by SYBO Games as a beta version of Subway Surfers 2.0 (released officially in ).
Basically it is the Subway Surfers game, but with some changes such as new menus, new selection of characters and boards that will be officially be released later in , improved graphics, a 3D map with the ability to play in several updates of the original game (you simply cannot play updates that have not received the improved graphics of the update), a Seasonal Hunting with prizes such as coins, mystery boxes, characters and boards that collect a certain amount of items that automatically increases with each reward during the game.
Gameplay
The object is the same of the original game that is to run as far as possible in a endless game world while avoiding randomly generated obstacles that require the player to either jump (slide finger forward), duck (slide finger backwards) and dodge the oncoming trains in a precise manner.
Power-ups
Coin Magnet : Attracts coins even if the player is not in its path.
Jetpack : Allows the player flight in the air, temporary invincibility and collecting many coins.
2X Multiplier : Doubles the player's multiplier (x10 = x20)
Super Sneakers : Allows the player to jump higher. Useful for collecting hunt tokens.
Power Jumper : Makes the player jump higher.
Headstart : Same power as the Jetpack except there are no coins and there are 3 powerups upon end.
Score Booster : Boosts the player's multiplier by 5, 6, or 7.
Super Mysterizer : Gives a player an exclusive mystery power-up (This can only be collected on mondays).
Playable Characters
Trivia
All icons from the original game have been redesigned.
This game is published only by SYBO Games .
All characters in the game's history were available for coin purchase in this version. ( Coco was the only one not available)
All hoverboards in the game's history were available for coin purchase in this version. ( Monster was the only one not available)
Unfortunately, This app was only available in Denmark, but soon it came to other countries such as USA and UK.

Zoe doesn't display her animation, but every 5 seconds she will display a random animation from Jake .

It's not able to play on the Landscape mode, as you'll not able to see anything.
When you choose Thunderbolt to play with and activate it, the both character and board will have broken animation, as the character seems to be have glitched running animation while the board will spin around itself.
Rarely, the loading screen just stuck for no reason and you can't solve this.

Trainbombing
Trainbombing is a 2009 short animated film by Bodie Jahn-Mulliner , Rasmus Hansen, Sylvester Rishøj Jensen and Rasmus Ustrup which was what served as inspiration for Subway Surfers . Rishøj Jensen and Jahn-Mulliner were originally film makers but would later found SYBO and make video games instead.
Summary
A young graffiti artist in what appears to be New York tries to tag a train but is caught by an inspector and his dog (what would later become Guard and Dog in Subway Surfers). The dog and inspector chase the tagger and this goes on until the dog bites the boy's backpack filled with bottles of spray paint which causes the paints to spray everywhere and become similar to a jetpack, (this would later become a powerup in Subway Surfers) it sends the tagger flying into the air while the dog still has grip on the backpack and the inspector is holding it's leash as they all fly around painting everything. The three then slam into a train, the inspector and dog are blacked out from this while the tagger gets up and finds out the jetpack spraying everywhere caused him to paint the whole train and create a colorful mural, he then improves it by adding his tag onto the train and leaves. The short ends with the camera panning out to show the whole graffiti.
Trivia
"Bombing" is a term used by graffitists that refers to the act of graffitiing the entirety of a train.
The short features the Guard and Dog from Subway Surfers but not any of the playable characters as the main character, instead an original character who has never appeared in Subway Surfers is used, he is a dark skinned boy with a red jacket, afro, and big lips.
He might of inspired the design of Ella .
His tag/nickname is probably King since this is what he writes on the train but his real name is unknown.
Coincidentally there is also a character in Subway Surfers named King , his name might be a reference to this short.
He might of inspired the design of Ella .
His tag/nickname is probably King since this is what he writes on the train but his real name is unknown.
Coincidentally there is also a character in Subway Surfers named King , his name might be a reference to this short.
Coincidentally there is also a character in Subway Surfers named King , his name might be a reference to this short.
The film seems to take place in Manhattan, New York as you can very clearly see famous buildings at the beginning of the film such as the Chrysler Building and the Empire State Building .
References

Trains
Subway Surfers is nothing without trains ! In real life, trains are a form of rail transport consisting of a series of vehicles that usually runs along a rail track to transport cargo or passengers. But in this game, they can be extremely harmful! Crashing into a train will end the player's run. Some trains move opposing the direction the player runs while most trains stay stationary. They cannot run through them, roll under them nor jump over them (unless they have the Super Sneakers , Double Jump, or the Pogo Stick ), but some trains have ramps where they can run on top of them to have a better view of what's to come, and have a chance to collect many coins , power-ups or even tokens for the Season Hunt . The trains' appearance will change depending on the current edition. During the release of each edition, the appearance of the trains would change, but there are always 3 types of trains per edition.
Trivia
Attempting to switch the player's character into a lane where there already is a train right in front of them will result in their character being slowed down, allowing the Inspector to catch up.
Doing so while the inspector is right behind them will result in getting caught.
Doing so while the inspector is right behind them will result in getting caught.
When they are in the place where moving/running trains are in the middle and they crashed in one of them (except the last and 2nd to the last train), the next train will still move/run and the coins will be left "flying".
In the editions from the end of 2021 onwards, the freight trains now have locomotives pulling them (except for ).
The locomotives seem to be based off the General Electric Evolution Series locomotives, though they do change depending on the location (ex: in Haunted Hood, the trains are based on the EMD SD70ACe, Union Pacific GE U50 "Baby Huey", and Santa Fe M-190 Doodlebug).
The locomotives seem to be based off the General Electric Evolution Series locomotives, though they do change depending on the location (ex: in Haunted Hood, the trains are based on the EMD SD70ACe, Union Pacific GE U50 "Baby Huey", and Santa Fe M-190 Doodlebug).
There are some default trains that are used if the update location does not have a default train to use. One example is 's trains, which have been used for many maps such as , , and . A new train was added in and was used for several other maps such as , , and . Another example is 's trains which have been used for special maps including , , , and , along with other cities including and .

Trespasser is a hoverboard released in . Like any other hoverboard, it protects the character from crashing for 30 seconds. When crashed, it will need to recharge for a few seconds before it can be used again.

Trick or Treat
Trick or Treat is a game mode released in for some users, where there were only 3 stages and no characters or boards as rewards. In , this game mode was officially released for everyone and had 5 stages and you need to complete all 5 stages to unlock Dr. Flyman . It takes place in , and Bob The Blob in their Slime Outfit runs through the subway throwing candies to you.
How to Play
Chase Bob The Blob .
Collect the candies he drops, avoid the rotten fish.
You have three minutes to pick up as many as you can.
Releases
Pro tip
Use a board with Super Jump and Smooth Drift powers to attract the candies. And avoid the rotten fish.

Tricky
Tricky is the deuteragonist of Subway Surfers . She is the first ever female surfer in the game. She is a character that can be unlocked by collecting 3 Tricky's Hat ( ) tokens. Her first outfit, the Heart Outfit, costs 150. Her second outfit, the Camo Outfit, costs 300.
Robert Gideon and Mrs. Fairchild are her parents.
Beatrice Fairchild is her real name but, the crew calls her "Tricky".
Source: Subway Surfers: The Animated Series
Appearance
Default Outfit
Tricky is a teenage light-skinned girl that wears black square glasses. She has a cropped white tank top, a black belt, light-blue low-rise baggy jeans that reveal some kind of red garment underneath. She also has blonde hair styled in pigtails, green eyes, and shoes that look like Jake 's.
She also has two other outfits , which are the Heart Outfit and the Camo Outfit. They cost 150 (formerly 300) and 300 (formerly 150), respectively.
Heart Outfit
In her Heart Outfit, she loses her glasses and wears turquoise ring earrings. She wears a cap and a white crop top with a purple heart on it. Her trousers also change to purple and she wears bracelets. Tricky also has purple brilliant lipstick. The original price for this outfit ( 40 ) was eventually changed, currently costing 150.
Camo Outfit
In her Camo Outfit, she wears black shades, a pale brown crop top, and camo trousers with black sneakers, with green laces. Her beanie changes to a slightly darker shade. The original price for this outfit ( 25 ) was eventually changed, currently costing 300.

Tricky's Hat
Tricky's Hat is a character token needed to unlock Tricky , her Heart outfit and Camo Outfit. It can be obtained withing the Token Box , Mystery Box or Super Mystery Box .

Tricky (Subway Surfers City)
Tricky is a revamped version of the Subway Surfers character Tricky . Currently in Subway Surfers City , she can be unlocked by reaching at level 9 of the XP Level.
Appearance
Tricky is a teenage girl with blonde hair styled in pigtails, green eyes and black square glasses. She wears a white cropped t-shirt with a red crown print, red tank top underneath, a white sweatband on her right arm, blue rolled-up jeans with a black belt, white shoes with green and red details and a red hat.
Power-ups
Style & Glow
Tricky gets a coin doubler when picking up 200 coins.
Terre Game on Point
Tricky gets a bouncy shield when picking up 300 coins.
Money Bags
Tricky gets a coin sack when picking up 125 coins.
Trivia
Her current design has made appearances in Subway Surfers Tag , Subway Surfers Match , and Subway Surfers Blast .
Her hoverboard is Naughty & Nice .

Trophies
Trophies are collectibles in Subway Surfers . They are quite rare, and can only be collected from mystery boxes . Other than showing how lucky players are, they serve no use in the game (until edition). They can find them at the 'Me' menu along with the characters selection and the hoverboards selection screen. At the start of the edition, trophies were able to be received again multiple times due to the new Spraycan Awards introduced at that edition. There are 8 trophies in the game:
Gemstone Description: It belongs in a museum!
Gold Bar Description: Directly from Fort Knox.
Classy Clock Description: Show your style like Flavor Flav!
Dollar Chain Description: Bring something bling to the party.
The Paperweight Description: A little Gold Sapiens.
Headphones Description: Groove is in the heart.
Platinum Record Description: Bring the beat back!
Cassette Tape Description: Retro musicology.

Trym
Trym is a limited character released in update. He costs 75. His Noble Newbie Outfit costs 31,000 in , now 150.
Appearance
Default Outfit
Trym is a Danish teenage boy from who wears a viridian tunic with a dark gray cape and a brown buckle. He has his sword on the left side of his buckle and has his wooden shield on his left arm. He has brown legging and leather boots, along with a skobleoff monster-themed helmet on his head.
Noble Newbie Outfit
Launched in , he has a patch on his face, his vest, helmet, shield and sword becomes cardboard, his long-sleeve shirt under his armor is black, his shorts are dark blue, and he also has golden yellow shoes, and white and blue socks.
History
* An exact date was found where he appeared in, otherwise, the edition release date is assumed.
Trivia

Trym is the second surfer, with the first being Freya , the third being Super Runner Jake , and the fourth being Catrine , but from as Rival .
Trym gets Multiplier Bonus for Multiplier Bonus in , , and .
For unknown reasons, he didn't receive his 1st outfit in  or .
He is the 13th surfer to receive an outfit in 2023 (in ), after George , Bonnie , Festive Frank , Rabbot , Pink , Phoenix , Koral , Noel , Callum , Cleo , Zayn , and Moira .
For unknown reasons, he didn't receive his 2nd outfit in .
Viking and Big Boss Board are his boards.
In , his Noble Newbie outfit's scrollbar pose has his normal outfit's sword and shield instead of his cardboard ones.
In the Chinese Version, his name is Viking (维京人) and his Noble Newbie Outfit is called the Guard Outfit .
His last appearance in the files was .
Also his name means Escape in Norwegian.
In the 3.42.3 version, his Noble Newbie Outfit became limited.

Tsuki Chan
Tsuki Chan is a character released in . She is the 274th character in the game and the 12th surfer of 2026 .
Appearance
Default Outfit
Tsuki Chan is a 16-year-old Japanese teenage girl from who headcarries a black cat, and has a blonde hair styled in a Odango hairstyle (two dumpling-like hair buns) with long twin pigtails and left-sided bangs. She matches her long-sleeved white and purple crop top with her white baggy pants and purple skirt belt, which are accessorized with red fingerless gloves, a pair of circular earrings (one of which has a diamond bead attached), and a giant back bow attached to the aforementioned belt. Her red sneakers also have some purple accents and star add-ons sticked on the sides.
Trivia
She and Kaito are the 12th and 13th surfers released in 2026 , after Frank Noir , Holly , Sean Snow , Mia , Rosalita , Lorcan , Horse , Vittorio , and Enzo , Sprinkle , and Bunny Betty .
She is also the seventh female 2026 surfer, after Holly , Mia , Rosalita , Horse , Sprinkle , and Bunny Betty .
She is also the second female 2026 character who costs , after Sprinkle .
She is also the seventh female 2026 surfer, after Holly , Mia , Rosalita , Horse , Sprinkle , and Bunny Betty .
She is also the second female 2026 character who costs , after Sprinkle .
She is the fourth surfer, after Harumi , Akira , and Yuto .
She is also the second female surfer, after Harumi .
She is also the second female surfer, after Harumi .
She reuses Ming 's animations.
She is the 56th surfer to reuse animations. The previous surfers were Frankette , J Balvin , Vivienne , Captain Flash , Clockwork Johnny , Thunder , Azura , Astra , Lucky , Red Panda , Faiza , Electra , Riley , Glorgalax , Ventura , Leo , Ozzy , Billy Bean , Kedi , Ruhi , Pumpkin Pete , Leif , Demi Lovato , Kaleo , Rani , Finlay , Stevie , Berta , Costa , Rochelle Rebelle , Asher , White Rabbit , Peter , Henry , Tara , Sunny , Alma Brava , Mateo , Crewmate Jake , Crewmate Tricky , Crewmate Spike , Crewmate King , Crewmate Tasha , Crewmate Guard , Rosie , Ballerina Latticina , Holly , Sean Snow , Mia , Rosalita , Horse , Vittorio , Enzo , and Bunny Betty .

Tumi
Tumi is the thirty-ninth limited hoverboard in Subway Surfers , released with . Like any other hoverboard, it protects you from crashing for 30 seconds. When crashed, the hoverboard will explode and need to recharge for a few seconds before using again. It costs 50,000 coins, like most of others limited hoverboards.
Appearance
This is a blue board with a golden Inca calendar. The Zap Sideways upgrade adds two green and blue teleporter things to the sides.
Trivia
Tumi is the fifth new board in 2016, first being Groovy , second being Jouster , third being Chameleon and fourth being Ray .
This is the 1st and currently only board.
Tumi was also used in a game called Tran Surfers which takes place in , where the bootleg version of Jake , now known as Jak , was discovered.
Tumi got an upgrade in called Zap Sideways , which costs 40.
In , it was for the Season Hunt for 100.
In , it cost 30.
Carlos and Tankbot share this board .
This board wasn't used in 2022 and 2023 for unknown reasons. This happened to many other boards after 2021 , but a lot of them had made reappearances recently.
In , it costs .
In , it costs in Offer Trails .
This is the last board to receive an upgrade outside its original release.
In the Chinese Version, it's non-limited board and it doesn't have upgrades.

Tutti
Tutti is a limited character in the update. He’s the 157th character in the game.
Appearance
Tutti is a 14-year-old invisible Castilian/Spanish teenage boy from . He wears a red cap with blue headphones on top of it as well as yellow sunglasses that show his facial expressions. He also wears a white t-shirt with red stripes and teal shorts with yellow lines on them. He also wears white socks with teal-colored lines on them and red slippers. His secret ice cream mesh cone is a brown waffle cone with yellow ice cream. His scooter in his animation is teal colored. In his Loading Screen version, his legs are visible.
History
* An exact date was found where he appeared in, otherwise, the edition release date is assumed.

Frutti is his sister.
He is also the second character to have a sister ( Frutti ), the first being Fresh ( Ella ).

He and Frutti are the sixth and seventh surfers to have headphones, the first being Jake (Star outfit), the second being Jia , the third being Sofia , the fourth being Dylan (Walkman outfit), fifth being Alexandre and the eighth being Hali .

Ukulele is the fifty-first limited hoverboard in Subway Surfers . It is a limited hoverboard exclusive to . Like any other hoverboard, it protects you from crashing for 30 seconds. When crashed, the hoverboard will explode and need to recharge for a few seconds before using again. Like most of the others limited hoverboards, it costs 50,000.

Other Unused/Upcoming Stuff
Trivia
The World Map is a future feature of the game, it was originally released in Train Riders (the beta game of Subway Surfers v2.0). The World Map has all cities in the game except old cities from the game like São Paulo, Washington D.C., Transylvania, etc, it also had 9 exclusive cities (Guangzhou, Macau, Harbin, Sanya, Chengdu, Guilin, Hangzhou, Xi'an, and Gansu) but they were removed in the version 3.0. Other old cities from Arabia 2017 to don't work since .
Season Tickets are already in the game since July 2021, but they have not yet been officially released.
Currently, when we collect Season Tickets, the Subtitle that appears suggests that it is a trophy of achievement, but it is actually a future currency.
The "Run The World" event appeared at the beginning of the new version of the game in , available when you traveled through time advancing the date, this event only appeared until and then was no longer available, at the time it revealed the character Qin who only it would be released later on .
The icon of the city of Wuhan was revealed by artist Mathilde Garreau who draws several concept arts for the game, in a ArtStation post she showed several city icons including that of Wuhan which is a city in the Chinese version of the game that has not yet been released in the Western version.
On a stream, Kiloo confirmed the New Year 2020 edition would be a brand new location in Africa, but  was released instead.
On a stream in SYBO ( 2020 ) said "We got Calli for Christmas" people thought Cali may mean California but we got instead.
During the update, in the localization file the following cities were leaked: Oxford, Houston, Copenhagen, Chang'an, Paris, Tokyo, Mumbai and Yellowstone, meaning the update after was supposed to be Yellowstone , but  was released instead. There was even a board with the subtitle of Yellowstone Board seen in the game files but was never seen in game.
The update was called Paris Summer Games , but due to some copyright of the Olympics the subtitle "Summer Games" had to be removed, the update completely changed the icon, the city icon, the graffiti, the logo, the scenery, in addition to Leo and Hélène becoming "Paris Surfer", and the update trailer was also replaced.
