Uprock
Uprock is a board exclusive to the edition. Like any other hoverboard, it protects you from crashing for 30 seconds. When crashed, the hoverboard will explode and need to recharge for a few seconds before using again. It costs 180 during the Season Hunt .
Appearance
It is a purple-colored board that has an artwork of a turquoise spray can with purple and red-colored stars on a yellow background.
Trivia
This is the 6th board of 2021 after Secret Society , Discovery , Endeavour , Crane , and Buddy Bear .
Uprock is the 3rd hoverboard, the 1st being Liberty , the 2nd being Yellow Cab , and the 4th being Color Shift .
So far, it is the last hoverboard to use the classic Hoverboard design.
Uprock returned in Season Hunt for Level 10.
If you look closely at the board, you will see a spray can.
For unknown reasons, it did not return in .
For unknown reasons, it never returned in 2023 .
In , it returned for free as a Thanksgiving gift on November 28th, 2024 .
It is Darryl 's and Lana 's board .
In the Chinese Version, it's called Free Graffiti (自由涂鸦) .

Valentina
Valentina is a limited character released in ,, who is the winner of the Valentine Vandals Craft-a-Character Contest. She was made by @paolapintando . She is the 218th character in the game and third surfer of 2025 .
Appearance
Default Outfit
Valentina is a 17-year-old American-Hawaiian teenage girl from , who has purple eyes and pink heart-shaped hair. For clothing, she wears a green, purple, and cyan colorblock shirt with a pink heart icon on it and a black crop top underneath. There is a simple black belt on her waist with a strap that holds a singular pink spray can. She also wears a blue baggy jeans with purple and green splatters on them, pairing with a pair of pink sneakers. Her accessories include an arrow headband, black heart-shaped earrings, and a purple and pink-colored respirator mask with heart shaped filters.
Pink Aphrodite Outfit
Released in , her heart-shaped hair now has a Greek spiral pattern in it, worn with a golden wreath of laurel. Her mask also changed into having a pastel pink base and dark purple hearts, coordinating the colors with her one-sided off-shoulder layered top, wrap pants, and sneakers. She finishes it off with her golden necklace, armlet, bracelet, and spraycan strap, which carries a pastel pink spraycan.

Vamos Dançar
"Vamos Dançar" is a song in Subway Surfers sung by Rebeca Roar . It was released in , on February 17th, 2025 . It's available in the game's boombox as background music and also in music streaming services, like Spotify or Apple Music. The soundtrack ( Vamos Dançar (Rio Carnival) ) is based on this song, but it doesn't have lyrics, only vocalizing.

Veggie Hunt
Veggie Hunt is a new gamemode released in , and for the time being exclusive to that edition.
How to Play
Collect all parts of the veggie burger throughout various parts of the game. Collect all ingredients to unlock Billy Bean .
Collect 9 lettuces ( ) during a run.
Collect 8 tomatoes ( ) from the mystery box .
Collect 4 avocados ( ) from .
Collect 4 veggie patties ( ) from the Season Hunt .

Veggie Patties
Veggie Patties was the fourth and final item that you needed to complete the Veggie Hunt and unlock Billy Bean during the update. You could get this in Season Hunt , and had to get 4 of them.

Ventura is a limited character released in . She is the 197th character in the game.
Appearance
Ventura is a 14-year-old American teenage girl from . She wears a white explorer hat with green and red arrows. She also wears a red bandana on her forehead. She wears a white shirt with 2 green straps on it, a red scarf and a brown and white jacket. Also, she wears white shorts with mud on the bottom. She wears oversized brown boots (one has a green vine entangled around it).

Version History
Subway Surfers is frequently being updated by SYBO ( 2012 -Present) and Kiloo ( 2012 - 2020 ). Updates are usually to add new Characters and Hoverboards and re-release existing ones, or to fix bugs. Updates are released every three or four weeks after the previous update's Weekly Hunt / Season Hunt ends, and Minor Updates are released every one week, and sometimes earlier. Minor Subway Surfers Updates are usualy to add or change features or fix some bugs that occured in the previous releases.
This list includes all Subway Surfers versions, some versions of the game may be missing in this list due to a lack of significant information or obscurity.
The current version of the game is 3.62.0.
List of Updates

Vittorio
Vittorio is a character released in . He is the 270th character in the game and the 8th surfer of 2026 .
Appearance
Default Outfit
Vittorio is a 2316-year-old ancient white marble human-shaped statue from . He has gigantic curly short hair akin to ionic columns with a line surrounding his forehead and a gap in between his hair to fit his dark purple headphones with orangish-yellow ear cups and ruby red triangles pointed to the front. His gray eyebrows start large and end small contrary to human eyebrows, and he has almond-shaped eyes with gray eyelines and pupils. He wears a shirt with long sleeves followed by a single thick yellow stripe crossing his shoulders and the side of his arms with a big text logo on his chest in ruby red somewhat spelling "Viv", loose mid length shorts, a toga wrapped around his shoulders and waist, leg warmers with a single ruby red stripe close to his sneakers and platform sneakers with dark purple outsoles. He also has a spray can or bottle on his waist at the back which has a dark purple sprayer/cap and ruby red wrapper. All parts of his body whose colors were not specified previously are white.

Vivienne
Vivienne is a limited character released in the update. She is the 173rd character of the game.
Appearance
Default Outfit
Vivienne is a 19-year-old British young woman/teenager from . She is dressed in a (red) golf shirt with black stripes and white fur on her shoulders. Brown shorts with two yellow buttons and a brown-yellow miniskirt in the back, white tights with black spots (like Dalmatians) and red boots. Her hair is short with white but one strand is black and the other side of the white hair is black. And she also has yellow eyes, black eyebrows and red lips.
Hollywood Glam Outfit
Launched in , she is dressed up as Marilyn Monroe. She has curly blonde hair styled in a bob cut and a beauty mark above her lips. She wears a white sleeveless dress with a red belt which is tied up in a bow, long red gloves with two gold bracelets on her left wrist and pearl earrings. Lastly she has red and white high-top dunks with white anklets on her ankles.
Night Hecate Outfit
Launched in , her outfit now embodies the Greek Goddess Hecate . She has a silk black high ponytail with a white streak and bangs, a blue-green eyeshadow, and headband that has a crescent moon positioned to look like horns. Her black halter top has a golden snake design that comes from her neck, perfectly paired for her long fingerless gloves with straps, blue-green shorts and kneepads, and black gladiator boots.
History
* An exact date was found where she appeared in, otherwise, the edition release date is assumed.

Wave Rider
Wave Rider is the thirtieth limited hoverboard in Subway Surfers . Like any other hoverboard, it protects you from crashing for 30 seconds. When crashed, it needs to recharge for a few seconds before it can be used again. The board is a limited edition hoverboard, exclusive to the . Like most of other limited hoverboards, Wave Rider costs 50,000.
Appearance
It has the appearance of a blue and white surfboard with a wavy pattern.

Wayfarer
Wayfarer is a limited hoverboard released with . Like any other hoverboard, it protects you from crashing for 30 seconds. When crashed, the hoverboard will explode and need to recharge for a few seconds before using again. Its special ability is Smooth Drift. Like most of the others limited hoverboards, it costs 50,000.
Appearance
It looks like a wooden boat with fabric flaps coming from the sides and back and a piece of rope in the boat.
Trivia
Wayfarer is the fourth board released in a China edition, the first being Panda , the second being Dragon , and the third being Rickshaw .
This board is a reference to the Hong Kong Junk , a famous boat in Hong Kong .

Wayne
Wayne was launched as a limited character in . He can be unlocked by purchasing with 100,000, just like some characters. He was also the 19th limited character in Subway Surfers, and the 17th limited character in the World Tour series. His 1st outfit, the Chief Outfit, costs 75, and his 2nd outfit, the Knight Outfit, costs 75.
Appearance
Default Outfit
Wayne is an American teenage boy from who dresses very similar to a cowboy, with a brown cowboy hat, a cowboy jacket, a sheriff badge, a black belt with a golden buckle, a pair of blue jeans, brown cowboy boots and a hobby horse.
Chief Outfit
Launched in , like all other limited characters introduced in 2014 (except for Jay ), he also had the Chief Outfit during his initial release, which costs 75. The Outfit makes him resemble a Red Indian, with his hat swapped for a tribal hat, his jacket for a chief jacket and his jeans for trousers. He also gained a red-eye mask.
Knight Outfit
The edition re-release gave him the Knight Outfit. He literally wears a knight's armor. This outfit also costs 75.
Trivia
Wayne is a reference to famous Western actor John Wayne . Both of Wayne's outfits are reminiscent of costumes from spaghetti westerns, popular yet low quality and poorly researched western movies that are usually filmed by Italians.
John Wayne "played" the Roman Centurion in the film The Greatest Story Ever Told. The Centurion resembles Wayne's knight costume.
John Wayne "played" the Roman Centurion in the film The Greatest Story Ever Told. The Centurion resembles Wayne's knight costume.
Buzz is his best friend (According to the official Friendship Day post).
They are representing the duo from the Toy Story movie by Disney & Pixar, Woody and Buzz Lightyear.
Woody has the same name's first letter (W) and they both are cowboys.
They are representing the duo from the Toy Story movie by Disney & Pixar, Woody and Buzz Lightyear.

Weekly Hunt
The Weekly Hunt , similar to the Word Hunt , was a new version of Egg Hunt from , where the player had to collect a certain amount of tokens to receive prizes. It was introduced during . The deadline for each challenge, as the title says, is one week (sometimes, the deadline is 2 weeks instead of 1, according to the update). The tokens needed to be collected can change depending on the edition (for example: in , Japanese Keychains are needed to be collected, while Pumpkins are needed to be collected in the update), however, they serve the same purpose. There are 4 challenges per edition (now it was has been changed to 3 challenges). Each challenge required the player to collect a different amount of tokens, the higher the amount the tokens needed, the better the prize. Weekly Hunts lasted for three or four weeks. However, from onwards, the Weekly Hunt was replaced with the Season Hunt .

Trivia
Unlike the letters for the Word Hunt and the Power-Ups , the locations for the tokens are harder to reach. Some appear on top of tunnels while others appear on top of trains. They occasionally appear on the ground.
Super Mystery Boxes , Wordy Weekend letters, and Hourglasses (later released) also appear in the same places as Weekly Hunt tokens.
Super Mystery Boxes , Wordy Weekend letters, and Hourglasses (later released) also appear in the same places as Weekly Hunt tokens.
Before the Weekly Hunt begun, the Egg Hunt in took its place.
For some reason, coins are not a reward in the Weekly Hunt (unless a Super Mystery Box contains coins).
The prizes the player can get are:

Keys
Limited hoverboards , like Cherry , Dragon etc.

Headstarts
Keys
Limited hoverboards , like Cherry , Dragon etc.

As of , the Weekly Hunt was replaced with Season Hunt .

White Rabbit
White Rabbit is a character released during . She is the 238th character in the game and the 23rd surfer of 2025 .
Appearance
Default Outfit
White Rabbit is a 17-year-old teenage girl from known for her fierce makeup look, red eyes, and blonde pigtails on red highlights. She has a rabbit headpiece, a pair of black gloves, a monochrome suit dress wrapped with a yellow belt and an enormous red tie, as well as a pair of asymmetrical socks and black shoes.
History
* An exact date was found where it appeared in, otherwise, the edition release date is assumed.
Trivia
She and Peter are the 23rd and 24th surfers released in 2025 , after Snake , Tang Yuan , Valentina , Kaleo , Rebeca Roar , Gabriel , Dinorange , Rocky , Rani , Finlay , Ribbon , Chicken Jake , Mallard Tricky , Oni Hime , Saru , LOC , Stevie , Berta , Costa , Yutani Tree Frog , Rochelle Rebelle , and Asher .
She is the 22nd surfer after Jake , Tricky , Fresh , Spike , Yutani , Frank , Ella , King , Lucy , Ninja , Tagbot , Tasha , Zoe , Brody , Prince K , Zombie Jake , Elf Tricky , Hammy-Bee , Pixel Jake , Ballerina Tricky , and Guard King .
She and Peter are the first and second surfers to not be a part of the Core Crew ( Jake , Tricky , Fresh , etc.) or one of their variants ( Zombie Jake , Elf Tricky , Guard King , etc.)
Hammy-Bee doesn't count because she's confirmed to be from Brazil.
Hammy-Bee doesn't count because she's confirmed to be from Brazil.
She's the second surfer whose name starts with W , the first being Wayne .
She idolizes Frank and makes rabbit graffitis based on him. Her appearance is also based on him.
Her name is most likely an alias to make herself feel mysterious, making her real name unknown.
She reuses Alia 's animations.
She is the 32nd surfer to reuse animations after Frankette , J Balvin , Vivienne , Captain Flash , Clockwork Johnny , Thunder , Azura , Astra , Lucky , Red Panda , Faiza , Electra , Riley , Glorgalax , Ventura , Leo , Ozzy , Billy Bean , Kedi , Ruhi , Pumpkin Pete , Leif , Demi Lovato , Kaleo , Rani , Finlay , Stevie , Berta , Costa , Rochelle Rebelle , and Asher .
She is the 32nd surfer to reuse animations after Frankette , J Balvin , Vivienne , Captain Flash , Clockwork Johnny , Thunder , Azura , Astra , Lucky , Red Panda , Faiza , Electra , Riley , Glorgalax , Ventura , Leo , Ozzy , Billy Bean , Kedi , Ruhi , Pumpkin Pete , Leif , Demi Lovato , Kaleo , Rani , Finlay , Stevie , Berta , Costa , Rochelle Rebelle , and Asher .
She is the ninth surfer of 2025 to cost real money, the others being Rebeca Roar , Rocky , Ribbon , Oni Hime , LOC , Costa , Yutani Tree Frog , and Rochelle Rebelle .
Business Time is her board .
She is the ninth surfer who is 17 years old, after Rain , Lawrence , Thunder , Skye , Astra , Audrey , Valentina , and Berta .
In her Meet The Crew biography, the zodiac sign and favorites section are covered by rabbit graffitis. This makes her the second character that has a tampered/undisclosed section at her Meet The Crew page, the first being Pins .
She is also the second character that has a biography but with an undisclosed zodiac sign, the first being Glorgalax .
She is also the second character that has a biography but with an undisclosed zodiac sign, the first being Glorgalax .
Her last appearance in the files was .
In the Chinese version, she is (Sky City) surfer and her name is Franco (芙兰可) .

White Rabbit Illustration Portrait
White Rabbit Illustration Portrait is a limited portrait released in the update. This portrait can be unlocked for 50.
Appearance
This portrait is basically White Rabbit but it is illustrated.
Trivia
This is the 22nd portrait released in 2025 , after Akira Illustration Portrait , Noodlebox Graffiti Portrait , Valentina Illustration Portrait , Pink Guard Graffiti Portrait , Rebeca Roar Illustration Portrait , Fantasma Graffiti Portrait , Yutani Graffiti Portrait , Rocky Illustration Portrait , Astra Graffiti Portrait , Chicken Jake Illustration Portrait , Soul Illustration Portrait , Meggan Graffiti Portrait , Oni Hime Illustration Portrait , Dog Graffiti Portrait , Stevie Illustration Portrait , Tricky Graffiti Portrait , Berta Illustration Portrait , Fresh Graffiti Portrait , Miss Maia Illustration Portrait , Rochelle Rebelle Graffiti Portrait , and Guard Illustration Portrait .

Wild Style
Wild Style is a non-limited hoverboard , released with . Like any other hoverboard, it will protect you from crashing for 30 seconds. When crashed, the board will explode and recharge for a few seconds before it can be used again. It costs 40,000.
Appearance
It has a shape of a regular skateboard with the "Subway Surfers" logo spray painted in cyan in the center.
Trivia
Wild Style is one of the 5 non-limited hoverboards launched in , the others being Crew , Surf & Spray , Blue Ice , and Crew Crush .
Its name (not counting Style ) is shared with Jia 's Wild outfit.

Wild Wednesdays
Wild Wednesdays was a weekly event, that started on Wednesday 12th March 2014, during , and . During the event, an already existing event will be held. See below for the events that took place at each Wednesday. It is similar to Mystery Mondays , but with a bigger variety of events. For copyright reasons, Kiloo had removed the option of tryouts. SYBO has removed Wild Wednesdays from the game in for an unknown reason.
Events during New York 2014 edition
Week 1 (12th March 2014) - Mega Jackpot Event

Week 3 (26th March 2014) - Lowrider tryout

Wind-Up Rocket
Wind-Up Rocket is a limited hoverboard released in . Like any other hoverboard, it will protect you from crashing for 30 seconds. When crashed, the board will explode and recharge for a few seconds before it can be used again.
Appearace
It is a red and gray rocket-shaped board, in addition to having 2 screws on the sides of the board, a clockwork on the back and a spherical tip on the front.
Trivia
Wind-Up Rocket, Flash Jet , and Snow Down are the last hoverboards from 2023 .
It is the fifth  board, the first being Choo Choo , the second being Glacier , the third being Ginger Boom , the fourth being Flash Jet , the sixth being Snow Down , the seventh being Buck Lightdeer , the eighth being Chill Out , the ninth being Candy Crash , and the tenth being Mean Green Machine .
It's the 24th board released in 2023 after Carp Diem , Ghastly Glider , Fan-tastic , Viperfish , I-Scream , Tagger , Danger Dasher , 11th Birthday , Flying Horse , Color Shift , Big Boss Board , Future Wave , Kite , Jamanta , Sweet Chili , Rave Rider , Flaming Doll , Back to the Future , K-Dillac , Superstar , Neo-Seoul , Retro Orange , Sharpeed , Fauxpunx , and Flash Jet .
Wind-Up Rocket is the 11th Christmas -themed board, the 1st being Snowflake , the 2nd being Jingles , the 3rd being Rudy , the 4th being Choo Choo , the 5th being Glacier , the 6th being Sleigh , the 7th being Ginger Boom , the 8th being Ho Ho Hoverboard , the 9th being Snow Cloud , the 10th being Wrapped , the 12th being Snow Down , the 13th being Jingle Dingle , the 14th being Shiny Surprise , the 15th being Gadget Force , the 16th being Ice Cool , the 17th being Buck Lightdeer , the 18th being Chill Out , the 19th being Candy Crash , and the 20th being Mean Green Machine .
Wind-Up Rocket didn't have its ability animation.
This was later fixed in an unknown 2024 update .

Windglider
Windglider is a type of hoverboard that protects from crashing for 30 seconds. When crashed, the hoverboard will explode and need to recharge for a few seconds before using again. It's the most expensive non-limited hoverboard in the game . It was introduced in the update.
Appearance
It has the appearance of a blue, white and red colored hoverboard formed with a glider on the bottom, with the wings coming from the bottom of the board.
Special
The special power of Smooth Drift gives the ability where when you jump, wings stick out and you will slowly glide back down to the ground for a few seconds, and when the super sneakers are activated, it will glide even higher, allowing you to glide over trains and other obstacles.
Trivia
It is currently the most expensive free hoverboard in the game, costing 360,000, with the second being Lowrider , costing 320,000, and the third being Hot Rod and Bouncer costing 280,000 each.
Its special power used to be called Air Drift .
It is Prince K ’s board according to the old Subway Surfers website
It used to have the same animation as Daredevil 's old animation however after the cling it just went to the default surf pose. This was later changed in where it got its own animation.
In the Chinese Version, it's called Web Glider (逐网) .

Woody
Woody is the thirty-second limited hoverboard in Subway Surfers . Like any other hoverboard, it also protects you from crashing for 30 seconds. When crashed, it needs to recharge for a few seconds before it can be used again. It makes its exclusive appearance in the update. Like most other limited hoverboards, it costs 50,000.

Word Hunt
The Word Hunt (formerly known as the Daily Challenge ) is a challenge where the player has to collect a certain amount of letters to form a word (i.e., Dash, score, jump, tie, roll, etc). The Word Hunt has a different word every day. When the player completes a word hunt, they will get a prize depending on how long they have been playing it.
Completing the hunt normally (or when the challenge is not played for a day or more) will give the player a .
Completing the hunt for 2 consecutive days will give the player a .Completing the hunt for 2 consecutive days will give them a .
Completing the hunt for 3 consecutive days will give the player 1,050.
Completing the hunt for 4 consecutive days will give the player 1,500, and completing it for 5+ consecutive days will give them a .
The player can see the letters they have collected on the Hunt tab (the tab which its icon has the current Season Hunt Icon) or when they pause the game.
Trivia
It was previously called Daily Challenge. The update renamed it to Word Hunt.
The Player will have a different word for the Word Hunt when playing on expired editions.
For example, if the player is playing in the update and has not updated the game yet, the words will be different than the current update.
For example, if the player is playing in the update and has not updated the game yet, the words will be different than the current update.
The 'Word Hunt' is an optional selection.
If the player does not want to complete the Word Hunt, they will have a lower chance of finding any power-ups unless they use a Headstart (except the Pogo Stick and Jetpack ) until they collect all the letters.
If the player does not want to complete the Word Hunt, they will have a lower chance of finding any power-ups unless they use a Headstart (except the Pogo Stick and Jetpack ) until they collect all the letters.
Like coins or other power ups, the letters can be collected when the character jumps over it while wearing the Super Sneakers , however it cannot be attracted by the Coin Magnet .
The words that the player needs to collect all have a connection to the game or ongoing events in the game/real life.
Sometimes the words connect with the current edition. For example: the words would be Halloween-themed during Halloween editions (e.g: or ) while it would be Christmas-themed during the Christmas editions (e.g: or ).
Sometimes the words connect with the current edition. For example: the words would be Halloween-themed during Halloween editions (e.g: or ) while it would be Christmas-themed during the Christmas editions (e.g: or ).
Sometimes, for new year celebrations like in and the word will not be a word but would rather be replaced by the new year number. For example, “2022” and “2023.” This also resulted in unintended behavior of every “letter” being A for the year number since the game doesn’t have a model for numbers of the year, so it just defaulted back to A.
Gallery

Wordy Weekend
Wordy Weekend is a event that was introduced in the edition (Week 45). It is 48 hours long and is held on Saturdays and Sundays. In , the feature was put one day early for unknown reason, however, in , it was again on Saturdays and Sundays.
At this weekend event players must find the letters of a character's name in the game. The letters are gold/yellow to not be confused with Word Hunts. They appear in the same places as Weekly Hunt tokens.
Completing a name gives them a Super Mystery Box , after which they can move onto the next character. If they don't have a character they're given the opportunity to buy them using coins at regular price, or they can skip the character by watching an advertisement. However, they can only watch the advertisement if you have a secure internet connection, or else they will have to use keys to skip to the next character. To check that their Internet connection is working, they can go to the "Earn Coins" menu and see if the sponsored video comes up. If it does, they must check the Wordy Weekend tab again and it should show a video icon under "Skip" instead of Keys. If it doesn't show this, they must try watching the sponsored video and then going back to the tab. The Wordy Weekend event is the first time they can buy limited characters with coins after the edition where the characters originally appeared (see pictures on the end of this article).
Character Order
The character order for Name Hunting seems to be based on a eight week cycle of the core 16 characters appearing in a predetermined order, 18 characters in Christmas Core ( Elf Tricky and Buddy included), and 17 characters in Halloween Core ( Zombie Jake included)
From Arabia Week 45 to Tokyo Week 76 only Core 1-4 were used, but in Tokyo Week 77, the Christmas Core was introduced. It was used every weekend from Tokyo Week 77 to North Pole Week 81. After edition arrived, Core 5-8 were introduced. The rotation of Core lists from Hawaii Week 82 to present goes in order 5,6,7,8,1,2,3,4... In Transylvania Week 123, the Halloween Core was introduced. It was used every weekend from Week 123 to Week 125. In Arabia Week 140, the Musical Core was introduced, the Movie core was introduced in Week 142. The Dance Core was introduced in Hawaii Week 151.
These are the Core lists:
The empty spots were filled in using a fixed rotation of 21 limited characters. Since there were only 20 empty spots one character wasn't used each weekend. In this rotation Zombie Jake , Elf Tricky , Jamie and characters released after didn't appear.
However this has been changed in . Marco , Nikos , Zuri , Mike , Jenny , Jaro and Aina were added to the rotation in Sydney Week 98. As the rotation of Core lists goes 5, 6, 7, 8, 1, 2, 3, 4, there are only 21 empty spots in Core lists 5-8 and 20 empty spots in Core lists 1-4, but there are 28 characters in the rotation, so 7 of them are not used each weekend when Core lists 5-8 are on and 8 of them are not used each weekend when Core lists 1-4 are on. Zombie Jake , Elf Tricky , Jamie and limited characters released after still do not appear in the rotation.
This has been changed again in the . Jia was added to the rotation in San Francisco Week 138, Carlos and Ramona were added in San Francisco Week 139, Bjarki and Lauren were added in Arabia Week 141, Malik was added in Bangkok Week 143 and Jolien was added in Bangkok Week 144. As the rotation of Core lists goes 5, 6, 7, 8, 1, 2, 3, 4, there are only 28 empty spots in Core lists 5-8 and 27 empty spots in Core lists 1-4, but there are 35 characters in the rotation, so 14 of them are not used each weekend when Core lists 5-8 are on and 15 of them are not used each weekend when Core lists 1-4 are on. Zombie Jake , Elf Tricky , Jamie and limited characters released after still do not appear in the rotation.

Thematic Wordy Weekends
Sometimes, thematic Wordy Weekends may be hosted. They are always the same Musical core list, the same Movie core list and the same Dance core list.
Movie Weekend
Dance Weekend
Musical Weekend
This is the order of limited character rotation:
Beginning with Rio Week 57 the order has been successfully predicted using these patterns. To predict the order for a given week, start by using the formula MOD (week - 45, 4) + 1 to determine which Core list to use. Next use the formula MOD (week - 45, 11) + 1 to determine the Starting Point on the list of limited characters, and fill in the blanks starting with the first empty spot on the Core list. For example, Sydney Week 60 is predicted to use Core list 4, and start filling the blanks at point #5 Noon. However, this only worked until Tokyo Week 77.

World Tours
In 2013 , in the update, a new feature in Subway Surfers was added: World Tour . Each some weeks, usually three and during Christmas four weeks, there is a new update in the game, either a new place or a re-release. Normally, World Tour are based in cities or countries, but there are some non-existing places in real life. The current World Tour is , released for the eighth time on July 13th, 2026 .

Wrapped
Wrapped is a hoverboard released in .
Appearance
Wrapped has the appearance of the 8th Birthday board but as a wrapped present. The bow part of it is green and the base of it is red and yellow striped.
Trivia
It is the 3rd variation of 8th Birthday . The 1st being 9th Birthday , the 2nd being 10th Birthday , the 4th being 11th Birthday , and the 5th being 12th Birthday .
Like the birthday board variants, this board is free and received as a Christmas present.
This board was released on December 24th, 2022 , but time zones vary which can make it release possibly on December 25th, 2022 .
In the game files, the board is called Xmas 2022 .
This is the 10th Christmas -themed board, the 1st being Snowflake , the 2nd being Jingles , the 3rd being Rudy , the 4th being Choo Choo , the 5th being Glacier , the 6th being Sleigh , the 7th being Ginger Boom , the 8th being Ho Ho Hoverboard , the 9th being Snow Cloud , the 11th being Wind-Up Rocket , the 12th being Snow Down , the 13th being Jingle Dingle , the 14th being Shiny Surprise , the 15th being Gadget Force , the 16th being Ice Cool , the 17th being Buck Lightdeer , the 18th being Chill Out , the 19th being Candy Crash , and the 20th being Mean Green Machine .
This is currently the only board that did not return yet.

Xtra Cool is a hoverboard released in . Like any other hoverboard, it protects the character from crashing for 30 seconds. When crashed, it will need to recharge for a few seconds before it can be used again.
Appearance
A hoverboard designed with stripes in different shades of green. It has a black baseplate and a blue metal handle in front, accompanied with a black grip.

It, Fire Starter , and Turbo Love are the fourth, fifth, and sixth boards released in 2026 , after Road Rage , Frankmobile , and Perfectoplasma .
It's the sixth board, after Jingle Dingle , Shiny Surprise , Gadget Force , Ice Cool , and Fire Starter .
It is Mia 's first board . Mia 's second board is Turbo Love .

Yellow Cab
Yellow Cab is a limited hoverboard released in . It has the appearance of a New York Checker cab. Unlike most of others limited hoverboards, it cost 30 instead of coins.
Appearance
It has the appearance of a stereotypical New York City taxi, minus the roof and wheels. As the name suggests, it's yellow, and it also has a black/white checkered pattern running along the sides and hood. On the hood, what seems to be the Chrysler logo is also depicted, and the windshield has a "TAXI" label on top of it.
Trivia
This is the second limited board, the first being Liberty , the third being Uprock , and the fourth being Color Shift .
An outline of the Chrysler logo can be seen on the hood.
This is the first hoverboard to cost keys.
It's Tony 's second board . His first board is Liberty .
As of 2022 , it is the only board that is in shape that is not a hoverboard .

This board resembles a yellow scuba tank with a black and white design. It also has a bolted iron on the front edge and a rotor on the back side, which emits a bubble trail. When Super Speed is enabled, it gains a pressure gauge. Equipping Super Jump adds a cylinder.

Yutani
"Tech genius and the gang's Q. Science is her obsession. Wildly imaginative and loves trying to create contraptions that have never been done. Personally, she believes she may not be of this earth - a theory her friends amusingly find completely plausible."
Source: Subway Surfers Official Website
Yutani is a character that can be unlocked by collecting 500. It is said that Yutani is the hardest character to get, as she requires the most tokens among the other token characters. Her second outfit Gadget Outfit costs 250.
Yutani Rodman may be her full name as she was adopted by Mully Rodman in the animated series.
Appearance
Default Outfit
Yutani is a Japanese-American teenage girl in a large alien suit, with the alien suit being green with spots at the bottom, with 2 antennae, 2 large eyes, and 4 arms. The upper arms are the real arms and the lower arms are just parts of the suit and can't be moved.
Gadget Outfit
In Gadget Outfit, Yutani appears without the alien costume, with her short hair with two buns and a purple wick, she wears a pink shirt, a red skirt, yellow boots (similar to Amy Panda Outfit boots) with different colored socks, a blue jacket, and turquoise gloves. Yutani wears an gadget backpack that moves two yellow arms with two claws each, looks like it has four equal arms in the Alien costume.
Limited Outfits (Variants)
Festive Yutani ( )
Pride Yutani ( )
Super Runner Yutani ( )
Elf Yutani ( )
Yutani Harlequin Toad ( )
Yutani Tree Frog ( )
Trivia
Among all the characters that requires tokens to unlock, Yutani requires the most tokens to be collected.
So far, she is the oldest character to only have one outfit.
Yutani received a new outfit called the Gadget Outfit in the update.

She happens to be of Japanese descent.
Originally, Yutani's gender was not clear. However, later in the animated series, it was revealed she is actually a girl.
In the animated series, Yutani's alien suit is somewhat different than it is in the games. In the series, the suit appears lighter and is more fitting to her body, while in the game, the suit seems heavy and appears large.
In the animated series, it is revealed she has a crush on Jake .
Yutani has custom animations when playing as her with the Gadget Outfit during the title screen.
Before every new update, she often gets +5 Multiplier Bonus. After update, an other limited character in that edition will replace her in Special Thursdays .

On the same Facebook post it is confirmed that Yutani discovered Bob the Blob .

Yutani's UFO
Yutani's UFO is a character token that is needed to unlock Yutani and her Gadget Outfit. It can be obtained withing the Token Box , Mystery Box or Super Mystery Box .

Yutani Harlequin Toad
Yutani Harlequin Toad is a limited character released in .
Appearance
Default Outfit
She looks like the classic Yutani , but now it's a Harlequin Toad costume. A yellow-green-blue costume with black spots and black-green frog eyes, with 2 legs and 4 arms (as we know Yutani ).
History
* An exact date was found where she appeared in, otherwise, the edition release date is assumed.

Yutani Tree Frog

Appearance
Default Outfit
Yutani 's costume now mimics the appearance of a Tree frog : having a lime and blue skin, with vermilion-colored eyes and limbs.

She is the sixth surfer, after Diego , Charlie , Tutti , Berta , and Costa .
She is also the sixth Yutani variant after Festive Yutani , Pride Yutani , Super Runner Yutani , Elf Yutani , and Yutani Harlequin Toad .
She is very similar to Yutani Harlequin Toad , as they are both frog variants.
She is very similar to Yutani Harlequin Toad , as they are both frog variants.
For the 2025 Green Game Jam collaboration, Yutani (in her Tree Frog outfit) introduced her new lab partner Froggy who is a red-eyed tree frog.
According to the video, profits from this outfit will be donated to the On The Edge Fund , which aims to protect overlooked species and habitats worldwide.

She is the seventh surfer of 2025 to cost real money, the others being Rebeca Roar , Rocky , Ribbon , Oni Hime , LOC , and Costa .

Yuto
Yuto is a limited character released in the update.
Appearance
Default Outfit
Yuto is a 14-year-old light-skinned Japanese teenage boy from who wears a red shirt with a skull design. He has dark shorts and dark shoes, as well as a dark wristband on his left wrist. He also wears a long sleeve on his right arm with dark flames, and he has long hair that cover his eyes.
Rock Out Outfit
Launched in , Yuto also wears the large red and blue-striped tie for the usual blue-accented red suit. He also has red Bermuda shorts, white high socks with a light-blue stripe on each pair, and a red, blue, and white sneakers. He still has his usual hairstyle, but he's now wearing a large blue cap with the UCC symbol on it.
History
* An exact date was found where he appeared in, otherwise, the edition release date is assumed.

He is the third surfer, after Harumi and Akira .

Yuto is the seventh character in 2023 to be available in Subway Studio , after Tainá , Bruno , Alfie , Spraybot , Frutti and Tutti .
In the animation, his hair rises a little and his eyes are visible.
He is the first surfer to have hair covering his eyes, the second surfer is Hamza . Curiously they look similar to each other.

Zap Sideways
Zap Sideways is a hoverboard upgrade. When equipped on a hoverboard, the character will teleport when they change lanes during a run. It's only a visual effect, as players can still stumble onto obstacles like light signals when changing lanes. Color Cloud has Zap Sideways, but that power was renamed Color Splash until an update in 2024 or 2025 which changed that.
List of Hoverboards

Zapline
Zapline is a hoverboard released in . Like any other hoverboard, it protects the character from crashing for 30 seconds. When crashed, it will need to recharge for a few seconds before it can be used again.
Appearance
It's a red, blue, and green slingshot with a curved handle and an orange rubber band.
Trivia
It, Trespasser , and Mizu Dash are the 17th, 18th, and 19th boards released in 2025 , after Hyper Viper , Lantern , Override , Fresh Love , Heartstopper , Dear Dart , Roarsome , Arara Board , Moongazer , Purple Spin , Cyan Note , Blue Riff , Eggscalibur , Bloomster , Edinburst , and Guard Board .
It is the third board, the first being Trespasser , and the second being Mizu Dash .
It is Thunder 's second board . His first board is Electro Shock .
In the Chinese Version, this board is (Suzhou 2025) board and called Flash (闪现) and it has Zap Sideways upgrade.
In and , it costs in the Offer Trails .

Zara
Zara is a character exclusive to Subway Surfers City . Despite being exclusive to Subway Pass , she is now occasionally available in the shop.
Appearance
Zara is a teenage girl with dark brown hair styled in a low ponytail and brown eyes. She wears a black tank top overlayed with a white crop top with red details, red pants, red sweatbands, a silver prosthetic leg with a red prosthetic foot, a white sock and a red sneaker with white detailings, silver hoop earrings, protective glasses with a deep red armature and she finishes off her look with a red bucket hat with a white arrow design.
Power-ups
Athletic Agility
Zara gets bonus score by jumping over or sliding under hurdles.
Hurdle Looter
Zara gets a coin sack by jumping over or sliding under hurdles.
Personal Best
Zara gets a bonus score multiplier of 0.1 every 30 seconds (max being 2).
Trivia
Zara is the 15th character in Subway Surfers City .
She is the 5th character exclusive to Subway Surfers City . With the first being Rosalita , second being Billy , third being Jay and fourth being Lilah .
She is the second character with a prosthetic leg in the Subway Surfers franchise, the first being Zayn .
She’s also the second marathon runner in Subway Surfers media the first being Jay .
Zara’s name is of Arabic origin.
She has 4 exclusive animations in game.
Most of them can be found in the menu: her intro, breathe and upgrade animations
The last one can be seen in gameplay, which is her running animation.
Most of them can be found in the menu: her intro, breathe and upgrade animations
The last one can be seen in gameplay, which is her running animation.
Unlike Tasha and Jaewoo , Zara was released with her own exclusive upgrade animation, instead of having a reused animation as a placeholder temporarily.
Zara’s power-ups are mostly related to hurdles .
She was the first character with hurdle related power-ups.
She was the first character with hurdle related power-ups.
Her hoverboard is Pouncer .

Zayn
Zayn is a limited character from . He can be unlocked with 2.99$. Now, he costs 75. His first outfit, the Stealth Outfit, cost 30,000 in (now 75), while his second outfit, the East Vest Outfit, cost 400 in (now 75).
Appearance
Default Outfit
Zayn is a German teenage boy from with cerulean-black hair in a bowl cut. He wears a white shirt with cerulean shorts. On the back of his shirt, it says "Sub Surf", like on the back of Callum 's leather jacket. He has a red scarf, black fingerless gloves, a prosthetic leg, a bandaged leg with a black sneaker with red laces, and two cerulean bracelets. His right arm is also bandaged.
Stealth Outfit
Launched in , he wears a black, cyan, and white hoodie with the hood on. His shorts become white and black and so does his one shoe. His prosthetic leg becomes cyan and scarlet and his gloves turn scarlet and black. Finally, he has a white gas mask on with a scarlet outline.
East Vest Outfit
Launched in , he wears a black beanie, he has indigo hair, he wears a orange vest, his mouth is covered, He has black Sleeves and Gloves, he has purplish baggy sweatpants, a silver prosthetic leg, His shoes are claret with pink laces.

Zi Hao
Zi Hao is a limited character from the update. He requires 210 . His first outfit, Vanilla Strawberry, released in for 32,000, now it costs 150.
Appearance
Default Outfit
Zi Hao is a Chinese teenage light-skinned boy from with cyan helmet and bubble jacket with navy blue gloves on and black pants. He also wears a white, purple, black, and gray shoes. He has dark brown eyes. He also wears long gray socks.
Vanilla Strawberry Outfit
Launched in , he now wears a red cap with an ice cream drawing on the back, a white sweater with an ice cream on it, dark gray pants, white gloves and black-purple flip-flops.

Unknown
The following surfers have unknown/undisclosed zodiac signs on their Meet the Crew biographies:

Zoe
Zoe is a character that can be unlocked by purchasing with 120,000. She is the 4th most expensive character and the 2nd most expensive female character in Subway Surfers . Her first outfit, the Curly Outfit, costs 30. Her second outfit, the Biker Outfit, costs 30.
Appearance
Default Outfit
Zoe is a zombie who has a choppy short purple hair and purple eyebrows. She has light green skin and milky white-gray eyes. Her outfit consists of a red checkered shirt tied up above the waist, blue jeans shorts with black leggings, and red cowboy boots. Her attire looks like one a cowgirl would wear.
Curly Outfit
In the edition, Zoe received her 1st outfit , the Curly Outfit, which costs 30. Her hair has been changed to curly, long and blonde with a purple headband, and her shirt has changed to dark purple. What appears to be a name tag appears on the left breast of her shirt. Starting from , she wears red lipstick in this outfit.
Biker Outfit
In the edition, she received the Biker Outfit, which costs 30. Her clothes change to black and more punk-like, and she wears a black full-head helmet with a purple visor and a flame pattern on both sides.
Trivia
Zoe and Zombie Jake are the only zombies in the game.
Unlike him, she is a non-limited character and she is a girl.
Unlike him, she is a non-limited character and she is a girl.
One of Zoe's actions from the character selection page is a reference to the actions done by the zombies in Michael Jackson's "Thriller" music video. The name of the animation is even having the word “Thriller” in it.

Zombie Jake
Zombie Jake is the first limited character made available in Subway Surfers . He most commonly returns in Halloween Themed Editions (except and for unknown reasons) but sometimes requires an in-app purchase. Zombie Jake can be unlocked by purchasing with 100,000.
Appearance
Default Outfit
Zombie Jake is basically Jake  except that he's now dressed up as a zombie. He has light green skin and wears a ripped up taupe hoodie sweatshirt, slightly ripped jeans, and a slightly ripped hat.
Serious Outfit
Launched in , in his Serious Outfit, he wears a ripped magenta jacket. His pants are now magenta and are now slightly over his shoes, which are now jade. He wears a vermilion bowtie, his hat now has 'Rebel' on it, and has facepaint on.
History
* An exact date was found where he appeared in, otherwise, the edition release date is assumed.
Trivia
He and Zoe are the only zombie characters in the game.
Unlike her, he is a limited character, he is a boy, and is a remake of an existing character .

He is also the first character to completely change his skin color in his outfit.
He is the first Halloween-themed character.
One of his animations is a little similar to one of Tagbot ’s animations.
If you didn't get his Serious Outfit in the hunt after it ended his Serious Outfit could be unlocked for 30 .
Zombie Jake is the 1st Jake variation. The second being Festive Jake , the third being Pride Jake , the fourth being Super Runner Jake , the fifth being Elf Jake , the sixth being Outatime Jake , and the seventh being Pixel Jake .
He is also the first Jake variation with new idles.

Zuper Zapper
Zuper Zapper is a limited hoverboard released in . It the fifth and final board of Super Runners , being one of the group's boards, and the board of Super Runner Fernando . It was re-released for the first time in for .
Appearance
The board has two main colors: blue and red, so the two colors standing for Super Runners . It has a little battery on the side. On the battery, a zapping icon is shown.
Trivia
It's the 17th hoverboard of 2022 , after Night Lute , Smoking Slime , Aero Fish , Moped , Ssskater , Mag-Neat-O , Boombastic , Trickster , 10th Birthday , Super Surfer , Crash Test , Rolls Rabbit , Spring , Astronave , Lunar Tiger and Atomic Tile .
It's the fourth board after Atomic Tile , Scarab , and Croc .
It's the fifth and last Super Runner board after Mag-Neat-O , Boombastic , Trickster , and Super Surfer .
In , it came back in a bundle offer for .
In , it costs 14.000.
In , it costs either or in a bundle for 200.
Unlike the other Super Runners boards except for Boombastic , this one did not reappear in the  update due to unknown reasons. Although every Super Runner reappeared, Boombastic and Zuper Zapper did not come back with the other boards.
It is currently the last board in alphabetical order.
For unknown reasons, this board never returned in 2024 .
Its latest appearance was in .
It's Super Runner Fernando 's first board .
In the game's leaked files, it's called Super Zapper.
In the Chinese Version, it's a board and it's called Electrical Tester (电测仪) , it's Super Speed upgrade is separated and it have +5 Score (exclusive upgrade).

Zuri
Appearance
Default Outfit
Zuri is a Kenyan teenage girl from who wears a red dress with blue, white, red, and gold pokot neck ring and a short afro. On her forehead, she has a yellow, green, and black headdress with white strings hanging over her forehead. Also on her face are red markings on her cheeks and on the top of her nose that means danger, daring, urgency and energy. She also wears yellow and blue earrings. There are bracelets on her wrists and legs.
City Outfit
Launched in , she has a modern haircut, pink lipstick and gold earrings. She wears a blue shirt which exposes her belly button, golden pants and pink, green and blue sneakers.
Trivia
She is the last female newcomer of 2015 . The second is Amira .
She may be Maasai, given her clothes and markings. One of her animations is also similar to a Maasai jumping dance.
She is the second African character, the first being Jasmine .
She is the second female African surfer the first being Jasmine , and after third being Aina , fourth being Salma , fifth being Hasina , and the sixth being Faiza .
She is the sixth character released in 2015 after Rex , Izzy , Amira , Marco and Nikos .
